issues.title: KB term update/delete still bypasses scope ownership
issues.summary: KB update and delete paths still mutate by raw `term_id` instead of first proving the row belongs to the requested case or bucket.
issues.impact: A caller with a known UUID can mutate or delete another scope's KB term.
issues.notes: live smoke update

issues.title: Legacy explorer delete is unscoped and more destructive than advertised
issues.summary: The legacy explorer delete route deletes by raw artifact UUID, uses a visibility-only read helper, and deletes chunks while calling itself a soft delete.
issues.impact: This remains a broad destructive path outside the newer scoped artifact delete flow.
tests_passed: `npm run build` — success with unrelated pre-existing warnings
issues.notes: Remaining gap: | Correction (2026-03-27): | Correction evidence:

issues.title: Mounted-path ingest still persists partial state inside one logical import
issues.summary: `/intake/from_path` commits batches of `IMPORT_FILE` work before the full import is known-good.
issues.impact: One failed ingest can leave durable partial jobs and partially persisted import state.

issues.title: Report queueing is still non-atomic
issues.summary: The `Report` row commits before the `REPORT_GENERATE` job exists.
issues.impact: The DB can contain a `QUEUED` report with no worker job attached.

issues.title: Several ops mutation endpoints are still global-only system controls
issues.summary: Pause/resume/prune/rebuild controls are not scoped by case or bucket.
issues.impact: One API key can pause or mutate all queued work and global runtime state.

issues.title: Preview security actions still drop bucket scope
issues.summary: Preview security URLs still omit bucket scope while preview reads are bucket-aware.
issues.impact: Bucket-scoped security actions still drift.

issues.title: Default Ops entry still routes users into a broken global scope
issues.summary: The live dashboard still opens `/ops`, and the page still defaults to `global_scope=true` when no case or bucket is present.
issues.impact: Users still hit a 403-prone default path unless `OPS_ALLOW_GLOBAL_SCOPE=true`.

issues.title: Mounted-path ingest can still create orphan case rows
issues.summary: `/intake/from_path` creates or flushes the `Case` before proving any systems were discovered.
issues.impact: Failed imports can still leave orphan case rows.

issues.title: Legacy thread surfaces remain globally enumerable by default
issues.summary: Legacy thread list and timeline surfaces still do not require explicit case or system scope.
issues.impact: Thread history remains broadly readable unless callers voluntarily scope themselves.

issues.title: Analyst report history is broken against the current backend contract
issues.summary: `ReportPanel` still calls `/analysis/reports`, but the backend only exposes `/analysis/history`.
issues.impact: The report history surface in the live Analyst page does not load.

issues.title: `ingest_single` can hide the original failure behind a second exception
issues.summary: The exception path still tries to log an ingest event with `case=None`.
issues.impact: The route can mask the real validation/import error.

issues.title: Batch-date parsing still creates duplicate logical batches
issues.summary: Intake routes accept full ISO datetimes while batch reuse keys on exact datetime equality.
issues.impact: One logical day can become multiple batch rows.

issues.title: Universal search still drops `system_id`
issues.summary: The universal/AI scope builder still resets `system_id` to `None`.
issues.impact: System scoping in dashboard semantic/AI search remains partly fake.

issues.title: Lexical search no longer uses its fallback path
issues.summary: `_try_ilike_search()` still exists, but `_lexical_search_items()` never calls it.
issues.impact: FTS failure now looks like `0` lexical results instead of degraded but usable search.

issues.title: Search/semantic fallback paths still swallow runtime failures as empty results
issues.summary: Several search helpers catch broad exceptions and silently return no results.
issues.impact: Broken pgvector/FTS/runtime state is reported to users as “no hits”.

issues.title: Unsupported analysis types still complete as pseudo-success
issues.summary: Unknown report types are accepted at the API layer and later marked `DONE` with `"Unknown report type: ..."`.
issues.impact: Invalid report modes are not rejected early.

issues.title: Translation still exposes a broken `nllb_remote` engine alias
issues.summary: The resolver still imports `.nllb_remote`, but no such module exists in the package.
issues.impact: Selecting that engine is a runtime failure.

issues.title: Health/readiness can claim success while startup invariants were skipped
issues.summary: Startup checks are disabled by default, while `/health` and `/health/ready` only verify DB connectivity.
issues.impact: The service can report healthy while schema/FTS safety checks were never run.

issues.title: Explorer tree access is still globally scoped by default
issues.summary: Shared UI helpers still call `/explorer/tree` without a case, and the backend returns all cases.
issues.impact: Reused tree helpers expose global case topology by default.

issues.title: Security summary remains a global aggregate route
issues.summary: `/security/summary` still exposes global totals without case or bucket scope.
issues.impact: Analyst-facing security state is still not consistently scoped.

issues.title: Case-scoped report history is still unreachable through the live route/UI contract
issues.summary: The backend history helper supports case-only scope, but the mounted `/analysis/history` route still requires `system_id`, and the UI only attempts history fetches when `systemId` is present.
issues.impact: Case-scoped report history cannot load even if the wrong `/analysis/reports` path is corrected.

issues.title: Report editing is still a dead-end surface
issues.summary: The visible Save control in `ReportPanel` has no handler, and the backend exposes upload/create flows but no report-update route.
issues.impact: Analyst-side report editing/persistence is not actually wired end to end.

issues.title: Analyst report submission still mishandles mixed-scope validation failures
issues.summary: The analyst page passes all queued artifact IDs into `ReportPanel`, but submission uses only the currently selected artifact's scope and then treats any JSON response as a report object even on non-OK responses.
issues.impact: Mixed-system or mixed-case selections can fail backend validation while the UI still enters a broken report state.

issues.title: Canonical-entity settings tab is still broken against the backend contract
issues.summary: The settings UI reads the canonical list as a bare array instead of paginated `items`, posts `name` instead of required `label`, and renders `c.name` even though the backend response field is `label`.
issues.impact: The canonical-entity admin tab cannot reliably list or create entities.

issues.title: OSINT settings tab is still broken against paginated response and field shape
issues.summary: The settings UI reads `/kb/osint` as an array or `documents` list instead of paginated `items`, then renders fields like `id`, `url`, `summary`, and `tags` that are not the OSINT document model's canonical fields.
issues.impact: The OSINT admin tab usually shows empty results or incomplete/misrendered document data.

issues.title: KB terms and candidate review tabs still collapse paginated results into empty state
issues.summary: `SettingsPage` still treats paginated KB terms and candidate endpoints as bare arrays, so the terms, alias-candidate, and entity-candidate tabs can show empty state even when the backend returns items.
issues.impact: Large parts of the shipped KB review UI appear empty even when the backend has data.

issues.title: Graph “Review KB/Queue” deep links still land on the wrong Settings surface
issues.summary: `GraphPage` links into `/settings?kb_tab=...`, but `SettingsPage` never switches `activeMenu` to `kb` from query params and KB loading is gated behind `activeMenu === 'kb'`.
issues.impact: Analyst "Review KB" and "Review Queue" links do not open the intended KB review surface.
tests_passed: `npm run build` — success with pre-existing warnings

issues.title: Office document page still blocks renderable legacy HTML previews
issues.summary: The Office page only enters workspace mode when `artifact.preview.has_html` is true, but that flag only checks `preview.html` while the backend HTML resolver will also serve legacy `rendered.html`.
issues.impact: Some Office artifacts are already renderable via `/preview/html/document` but still show “HTML document view is not ready.”

issues.title: CIM snapshot and temporal detail routes still bypass case-scope enforcement
issues.summary: Several CIM detail routes load records by raw UUID and skip the `_require_case_scoped_detail()` helper used elsewhere in the router. Snapshot compare/restore and actor-timeline can therefore cross case boundaries whenever the CIM API is enabled.
issues.impact: The feature-gated CIM surface still has inconsistent case-boundary enforcement on high-value detail endpoints.

issues.title: Graph search, stats, and node-neighborhood routes still bypass `ENFORCE_CASE_AUTH`
issues.summary: The graph router enforces `case_id` under `ENFORCE_CASE_AUTH` only for `/graph/artifact/{artifact_id}`. `/graph/search`, `/graph/stats`, and `/graph/node/{node_id}` remain callable without required case scope.
issues.impact: The graph surface still exposes global cross-case topology unless callers voluntarily scope themselves, which is inconsistent with the strict graph access check already present on the artifact graph route.

issues.title: Auxiliary search routes still cannot be bucket-scoped
issues.summary: `/search/ask` and `/search/compare` still accept case/system scope only. They do not expose `bucket_id`, even though the main search routes and hybrid retrieval logic support it.
issues.impact: Analysts using auxiliary search surfaces can still fall out of bucket scope and compare or ask across a wider evidence set than intended.

issues.title: Analyst graph tab still drops bucket scope on the mounted GraphPanel
issues.summary: The analyst page mounts `GraphPanel` with `caseId` and `systemId` only. It does not pass the selected artifact’s `bucketId`, so the graph fetch widens beyond the active bucket when bucket governance is in use.
issues.impact: The live analyst graph tab can show a broader artifact neighborhood than the bucket-scoped evidence selection the analyst is reviewing.

issues.title: GraphPanel can retain the previous artifact’s graph after a failed fetch
issues.summary: `GraphPanel` only updates state on `res.ok` and only clears nodes/links when there is no selected file. A 403/500 while switching artifacts can therefore leave the previous artifact’s topology on screen.
issues.impact: A failed or denied graph request can leave stale topology visible for the wrong artifact, which is especially unsafe around scope/auth failures.

issues.title: Artifact detail reads still mutate state by queuing preview work
issues.summary: Preview/status read paths still enqueue `PREVIEW_GENERATE` and commit work.
issues.impact: Read access still has write side effects.

issues.title: Preview still fans out `TEXT_EXTRACT` and `OCR_QUICK` together
issues.summary: Preview success still queues both extract and OCR for many document classes.
issues.impact: Duplicate work and racey follow-on behavior remain.

issues.title: OCR fallback still bypasses the Tesseract feature flag
issues.summary: Low-confidence OCR fallback calls the private Tesseract path directly.
issues.impact: Tesseract can still run when runtime flags say it should not.

issues.title: OCR fallback accepts replacements without proving quality gain
issues.summary: Fallback is accepted when `t_layout` exists even if measurable confidence gain is missing.
issues.impact: OCR output can be swapped without evidence it improved.

issues.title: Search ranking still penalizes Urdu/WhatsApp-heavy evidence
issues.summary: Hybrid search explicitly marks `urdu inpage`, `whatsapp`, and `screenshot` artifacts as noise/penalty terms.
issues.impact: Legitimate evidence is systematically down-ranked.

issues.title: Bucket-scoped graph entity search is still slow and incomplete
issues.summary: Canonical entity search scans batches then post-filters by bucket with a capped scan window.
issues.impact: Valid in-scope entities can be missed and query cost grows with corpus size.

issues.title: Graph node search still has an N+1 degree calculation
issues.summary: Node degree is computed as `len(outgoing) + len(incoming)` after loading nodes.
issues.impact: Graph search gets slower as node count grows.

issues.title: Explorer search still degrades to repeated backend paging plus Python post-filtering
issues.summary: When payload/artifact filters apply, explorer loops over retrieval pages and filters in Python.
issues.impact: Narrow searches are slow and pagination semantics drift.

issues.title: NER is still forced to CPU on Apple hardware
issues.summary: MPS is intentionally disabled and the token-classification pipeline is forced to CPU.
issues.impact: Apple Silicon deployments pay avoidable NER latency.

issues.title: NER remains structurally slow around the model itself
issues.summary: The NER path rescans text repeatedly, does DB-backed canonicalization in the inner loop, then does per-node and per-edge DB checks.
issues.impact: The bottleneck is pipeline architecture, not just model speed.

issues.title: Remote embeddings are still unbatched and lossy
issues.summary: Remote embedding generation samples chunks, embeds each chunk in a separate blocking call, then averages them.
issues.impact: Large documents lose coverage and always incur multiple sequential network round trips.

issues.title: Embedding validation still overstates success
issues.summary: Retrieval-layer embedding validation returns `True` when no retrieval-family rows exist at all.
issues.impact: Ops and job status can say embeddings are valid when search-grade vectors are missing.

issues.title: Ops monitor overcounts embedding readiness
issues.summary: Ops telemetry counts only `Artifact.embedding`, not retrieval-family chunk embeddings.
issues.impact: The dashboard can say artifacts are embedded while semantic search vectors are incomplete.

issues.title: `/keylogs/summary` still performs synchronous vault scans on the request path
issues.summary: The endpoint can open and parse up to 5,000 vault files per request.
issues.impact: This path is slow and easy to overload.

issues.title: Roadmap docs still overstate the transformer registry as an executable O4/mobile substrate
issues.summary: O4 docs and ADR-0002 describe a schema-validating transform runtime and mobile-engine foundation, but the code registry is a lightweight metadata loader used mainly for version/event decoration and missing-transform checks.
issues.impact: The docs currently make O4/mobile orchestration look more implemented than it is.

issues.title: Gap-analysis summary still overstates GIS readiness
issues.summary: The summary table marks PostGIS geometry as `Done`, but current code only provides geometry columns and no GIS router, service, geocoder, or geo-extraction processors.
issues.impact: The summary view still makes GIS look more operational than the codebase actually is.

issues.title: Gap-analysis reporting summary is cleaner than runtime truth
issues.summary: The docs mark reporting as implemented, but live reporting still has a broken UI contract, non-atomic queueing, and pseudo-success handling for unknown report types.
issues.impact: The roadmap status compresses a still-broken platform surface into a “done” bucket.

issues.title: O3 summary still overstates `CaseSnapshot` as done
issues.summary: The O3 summary table marks `CaseSnapshot` as done, but restore is destructive and only rebuilds a subset of captured state.
issues.impact: Snapshot support is present, but restore fidelity is not complete enough to justify a clean `Done`.

issues.title: O3 summary understates current identity-resolution correctness risk
issues.summary: The docs summarize entity resolution as partial because `IdentityResolutionLog` exists, but the engine still writes merge logs with `source_actor_id=None`, which makes the implementation riskier than the summary implies.
issues.impact: O3 identity resolution is not just incomplete; it still carries correctness hazards in the existing implementation.

issues.title: O1 docs still claim lexical search falls back to `ILIKE`
issues.summary: The O1 map still says search falls back to `ILIKE`, but the active lexical path only uses FTS and returns empty results when FTS is unavailable.
issues.impact: The docs overstate degraded-search resilience.

issues.title: Search scoping is still documented as cleaner than runtime truth
issues.summary: Agent-principles docs say search uses case/system/batch scoping by default, but universal search still drops `system_id`.
issues.impact: The roadmap overstates search-scope discipline.

issues.title: Security-gate docs still overstate automatic `REJECTED` triage outcomes
issues.summary: Several docs say `MALWARE_TRIAGE` finalizes `PASSED | QUARANTINED | REJECTED`, but the automatic path only sets `PASSED` or `QUARANTINED` unless `REJECTED` is already present.
issues.impact: The documented security-state model is stronger than the runtime currently auto-produces.

issues.title: Raw-text ownership is still cleaner in docs than in contracts
issues.summary: Docs say `TEXT_EXTRACT` is the canonical raw-text writer, but contracts still let `INDEX_NOW` write `chunk:0` and `PREVIEW_GENERATE` still claims `files:text.md`.
issues.impact: The docs overstate text-layer ownership consolidation.

issues.title: O1 preview/extract docs still describe a cleaner OCR ordering than runtime
issues.summary: The O1 map says OCR happens after extraction decides native text is insufficient, but preview still fans out extract and OCR together for many docs.
issues.impact: The current docs still hide duplicated extract/OCR fanout.

issues.title: Health/readiness is still overclaimed as an operational guardrail
issues.summary: Docs mark health surfaces as implemented, but startup invariant checks are optional and health probes only check DB liveness.
issues.impact: Docs overstate what “healthy/ready” actually proves.

issues.title: Classification docs still make the current classifier sound more meaningful than it is
issues.summary: Docs correctly say classification is partial, but still present it as a real surface without capturing that the visible label is largely rule-driven doc-type output.
issues.impact: Documentation still overstates current analyst-facing classification quality.

issues.title: OOXML canonical-text doc is still ahead of runtime truth
issues.summary: The OOXML indexing note is accepted as implementation direction, but contracts still allow `INDEX_NOW` and `PREVIEW_GENERATE` to own parts of `text.md` / `chunk:0`.
issues.impact: The accepted indexing discipline is not yet fully realized in code.

issues.title: Analyst-scope documentation is still cleaner than runtime behavior
issues.summary: Docs present analyst review/scope surfaces as implemented, but preview security actions still drop bucket scope and the default Ops path still falls into broken global scope.
issues.impact: Docs still overstate how consistently analyst-facing scope is enforced.

issues.title: O1 docs still overstate startup failure as a normal degraded-mode path
issues.summary: The O1 map says the API “runs in degraded mode” if startup DB work fails, but startup tasks are disabled by default and, when enabled, failures raise by default unless strict startup is explicitly disabled.
issues.impact: Docs describe an opt-in edge case as if it were the standard runtime startup behavior.

issues.title: Health documentation still collapses distinct probe semantics
issues.summary: Docs describe health as `/health` plus `/api/health`, but the mounted runtime also exposes `/health/live`, `/health/ready`, `/healthz`, and `/api/healthz`, each with different semantics and failure behavior.
issues.impact: Current docs hide the actual liveness/readiness/diagnostic split and over-compress what each probe proves.

issues.title: Blueprint still overstates `/api/cim` as a mounted runtime surface
issues.summary: The blueprint lists `/api/cim` under implemented O3 runtime status, but the router is only mounted when `ENABLE_CIM_API=true`, and the config default is `false`.
issues.impact: The docs still make CIM look like a default mounted API surface rather than a feature-flagged one.

issues.title: Reporting workflow is still documented as “partial” when core save/history behavior is broken
issues.summary: The blueprint frames reporting/workspace persistence as partial, but the live report UI still calls the wrong history path and exposes a Save control with no backend update path.
issues.impact: This is more than polish debt; the current reporting surface is not operationally complete.

issues.title: “Timeline polish” still overstates the current analyst timeline surface
issues.summary: The blueprint describes timeline behavior as a polish-level gap, but the live analyst page only renders a client-side artifact-date plot and does not consume backend timeline APIs.
issues.impact: The present analyst timeline is much thinner than the docs imply.

issues.title: Office workspace copy still overstates OOXML-layout behavior
issues.summary: The Office page says the workspace will render “the OOXML layout layer,” but the current implementation is only an iframe shell around sanitized HTML and does not load the separate layout endpoint.
issues.impact: The current UI promise is stronger than the runtime behavior actually delivered by the Office workspace path.

issues.title: Heavy image-intelligence routing emits follow-ons that the worker never schedules
issues.summary: `infer_image_intelligence()` emits follow-ons like `TRANSLATE` and `TIMELINE_REVIEW`, but the processing handler only consumes OCR routing and optional `FACE_DETECT`. The richer routing hints stay inert.
issues.impact: Screenshot/image evidence can be marked as needing translation or timeline review without any downstream job or review surface actually being triggered.

issues.title: OOXML document timestamps are parsed but never reach temporal enrichment
issues.summary: The OOXML parser captures created/modified document properties, but the projection object does not surface them and `enrich_artifact()` only passes filesystem/import metadata into `year.py`. Office document date inference can therefore miss the best available authored timestamps.
issues.impact: Office artifacts can fall back to batch date, ingest time, or content heuristics even when the parser already recovered stronger document-created/document-modified timestamps.

issues.title: Face detection is hardwired to CPU execution
issues.summary: The optional InsightFace face-detection path initializes `FaceAnalysis` with `providers=["CPUExecutionProvider"]`, so it cannot take advantage of available acceleration backends.
issues.impact: The first shipped FRS slice stays slower than necessary and can become a bottleneck on screenshot-heavy cases even when better execution providers exist.

issues.title: AI search answers are downgraded unless they contain the literal `[E` citation marker
issues.summary: The AI answer path treats any LLM response without `[E` as low quality and replaces it with `fallback_summary()`, even if the answer is otherwise correct and grounded.
issues.impact: Good AI answers can be silently discarded and replaced with generic keyword summaries because of a brittle formatting check rather than actual answer quality.

issues.title: AI-neighbor snippets are still query-agnostic and can mismatch the retrieved evidence
issues.summary: Hybrid/AI-neighbor results still use `_snippet_for_artifact()`, which picks the first preferred chunk by layer/order instead of extracting a query-relevant window for the retrieved concept.
issues.impact: Semantic neighbors can surface with snippets that are unrelated to the user’s query, which weakens trust in both ranking and citation quality.

issues.title: Translation still skips on unsupported selected engines instead of exhausting the documented backend decision set
issues.summary: After resolving one backend, the translation path immediately skips if that backend does not support the source language; it does not try the broader Marian/NLLB/IndicTrans2 decision set described in the roadmap docs.
issues.impact: Translatable documents can be marked as skipped simply because the first selected backend lacks support, even though another documented backend may have handled the source.

issues.title: Bangla and Urdu default translation still silently rewrite `TranslateGemma` to Marian
issues.summary: When no explicit engine is requested, the default `translategemma` lane is silently replaced with Marian for Bangla and Urdu if Marian supports the source.
issues.impact: Runtime behavior drifts from the documented primary-lane policy, making translation results and ops telemetry harder to reason about.

issues.title: Ops can report a different translation engine than the worker actually executes
issues.summary: The ops runtime snapshot returns the raw `translate_engine` flag/settings value, but the worker resolver silently maps unknown engine names to the IndicTrans2 backend.
issues.impact: A malformed runtime flag or config can make ops/UI show one engine while translation jobs actually run another, which undermines backend observability and debugging.

issues.title: Search answer citations still drop chunk-level provenance
issues.summary: Search evidence items carry `chunk_id`, but the final `/search/unified` citation payload omits it and only returns artifact-level references.
issues.impact: The answer layer is not fully traceable back to exact evidence refs, which is weaker than both the roadmap provenance contract and the internal evidence model.

issues.title: Artifact translation variants omit current non-canonical engines such as `parser_projection` and `indictrans2_fallback`
issues.summary: Artifact detail only builds translation variants from `KNOWN_TRANSLATION_ENGINES`, so current outputs produced under `parser_projection` or `indictrans2_fallback` are not surfaced as variants even when they are the active translation engine.
issues.impact: Preview/detail surfaces can hide the actual current translation variant and lineage state, especially on fallback or projection-based paths.

issues.title: Graph evidence is still artifact-only, not chunk/page traceable
issues.summary: Graph edges only store `artifact_id`, and the graph APIs/UI surface that artifact UUID as the sole evidence handle. There is no chunk/page/position provenance on graph relations.
issues.impact: The graph layer remains weaker than the roadmap’s evidence-ref contract because analysts cannot trace graph relationships back to exact supporting passages or page locations.

issues.title: Artifact detail and translation-variants routes disagree on the current engine
issues.summary: Artifact detail falls back from `triage_result.translation_engine` to `analysis_result.translation_engine`, but the dedicated `/translation/variants` route only checks triage.
issues.impact: The same artifact can report different “current” translation engines depending on which endpoint the UI calls, which makes lineage and variant selection drift-prone.

issues.title: GraphPage collapses chunk-level evidence selections into artifact-level graph context
issues.summary: Evidence search results are keyed with `artifact_id` plus optional `chunk_id`, but selecting a result always loads `/graph/artifact/{artifact_id}` and the selected-row state also keys on `artifact_id` alone.
issues.impact: Distinct evidence snippets from the same artifact collapse into one graph context, so analysts lose which exact passage justified the graph exploration.

issues.title: PreviewPanel can mark an unavailable translation variant as the active engine
issues.summary: When a user selects a translation engine, the UI sets `active_translation_engine` to that engine even if `/translation?engine=...` returns unavailable or null content.
issues.impact: The preview can show a fallback/projection engine as “active” even though there is no readable translation payload for that variant, which misleads analysts about what output they are reviewing.

issues.title: Search profile-compare overlap is artifact-biased and hides chunk-level differences
issues.summary: The `/search/compare` overlap key prefers `artifact_id` and never uses `chunk_id`, even though the compared retrieval items often carry chunk-level evidence.
issues.impact: Embedding-profile comparisons can overstate overlap and hide materially different evidence selections when both profiles hit different passages inside the same artifact.

issues.title: Unscoped graph stats ignore artifact visibility filters
issues.summary: `get_graph_statistics()` only applies `_apply_edge_scope()` when `case_id` or `bucket_id` is present. In the default unscoped path it counts raw nodes/edges directly and skips artifact visibility filtering.
issues.impact: “All cases / all buckets” graph stats can overcount or leak graph material that the scoped graph surfaces would hide.

issues.title: Scoped graph node search still reports global degree, not scoped degree
issues.summary: After finding scoped node IDs, `search_nodes_by_label()` computes degree from `len(n.outgoing) + len(n.incoming)` without reapplying case or bucket scope.
issues.impact: Analysts can see inflated connectivity counts that include relationships outside the selected case or bucket.

issues.title: Graph routes do not validate `case_id`, so malformed scope can 500
issues.summary: The graph router validates `bucket_id` only. The service then casts `case_id` with `UUID(case_id)` directly, so invalid case IDs can bubble into server errors instead of a clean 400.
issues.impact: A stale or malformed `case_id` query parameter can turn a bad client request into a server-error path and opaque empty-state behavior in the UI.

issues.title: Artifact graph payload still does not satisfy the mounted graph UI contract
issues.summary: `get_artifact_ego_network()` emits edges without `artifact_id` and only returns graph nodes derived from stored edges, while mounted graph UIs expect artifact-centric evidence labels and target-artifact highlighting semantics.
issues.impact: Artifact-edge evidence labels never render and the mounted graph views cannot reliably distinguish the selected artifact from its neighboring graph entities.

issues.title: LandingPage still reads the old queue-stats payload shape
issues.summary: The live `/home` page still computes queue depth from `high/default/low`, but `/stats/queue` now returns `total_queued`, `total_running`, and `queue_depth`.
issues.impact: The landing-page queue metric is wrong and will usually read as zero even when jobs are queued.

issues.title: PreviewPanel mislabels `stale_low_quality_text` translations as “Missing”
issues.summary: Artifact detail can return `preview.translation_state = stale_low_quality_text`, but the UI label helper only recognizes `stale_layout_only` and `stale_legacy_text`.
issues.impact: Low-quality stale translations are surfaced as “MISSING” instead of a stale/regenerate-required state, which weakens analyst guidance and hides the actual remediation path.

issues.title: Graph-to-Settings KB review links still drop bucket scope
issues.summary: `GraphPage` deep links into `/settings?kb_tab=...` only preserve `case_id`, and `SettingsPage` only persists `case_id` for KB review state. Bucket-scoped graph review can therefore widen once the user lands in Settings.
issues.impact: Analyst KB review links can still lose bucket scope and broaden candidate/term review beyond the active bucket context.
tests_passed: `npm run build` — success with unrelated pre-existing warnings

issues.title: Explorer restores arbitrary persisted search modes and can poison `/explorer/search` with invalid `mode`
issues.summary: `ExplorerPage` restores `mode` from URL/local storage without validation and passes it straight through to `ExplorerTable`, but `/explorer/search` only accepts `lexical|ai`.
issues.impact: A stale or malformed persisted mode can push the mounted explorer into backend `422` errors and leave search broken until the URL/storage state is repaired.

issues.title: Graph restores arbitrary persisted search modes and silently degrades invalid ones into lexical search
issues.summary: `GraphPage` restores any `mode` from URL/local storage, but its execution path maps every non-`entity` non-`ai` mode to lexical search while the UI buttons only recognize `entity|lexical|ai`.
issues.impact: Invalid persisted graph modes yield lexical evidence results with no active mode reflected in the controls, which makes the page state misleading and hard to recover.

issues.title: Shared semantic/intelligence search helpers still accept `systemId` but always strip it from non-lexical requests
issues.summary: The shared UI search helpers expose a `systemId` argument, but `runSemanticSearch`, `runUnifiedSearch`, and the non-lexical branches of `runIntelligenceSearch` force `includeSystem: false`, so callers cannot actually send system scope through those paths.
issues.impact: The helper contract overpromises scoping and makes system-aware UI controls partly cosmetic on semantic/unified search paths even before backend route drift is considered.

issues.title: Semantic search by `artifact_id` still loads the source artifact outside the requested scope
issues.summary: The vector-search path resolves the query/source artifact with `get_visible_artifact_for_read(db, artifact_id)` before applying requested case/system/batch/bucket scope. A caller can therefore seed semantic retrieval from an artifact outside the requested scope.
issues.impact: Search results may still be filtered, but the query seed itself is cross-scope, which weakens scope isolation and leaks whether an out-of-scope artifact exists and has embeddings.

issues.title: `/search/similar/{artifact_id}` still resolves the target artifact outside requested scope
issues.summary: The near-duplicate route loads the target artifact by raw UUID with visibility-only checks, then applies optional case/system filters only to candidate matches.
issues.impact: A caller can probe cross-scope artifacts as similarity anchors and infer family/simhash availability even when the requested result scope is narrower.

issues.title: PreviewPanel’s “Similar Signals” panel is still bucket-blind and mismatched to the `/search/similar` payload
issues.summary: The preview-side SimHash panel only sends `case_id` and `system_id`, not `bucket_id`, and then renders fields like `id`, `case_name`, and `system_name` that the backend similarity route does not return.
issues.impact: The mounted similarity panel can widen beyond bucket scope, never highlights matching artifacts in the graph view, and usually renders “Unknown Case / Unknown System” because the UI expects fields the route does not provide.

issues.title: Analyst queue reconciliation can overwrite correct artifact sizes with zero
issues.summary: Explorer list/search payloads prefer `file_size` and fall back to `size_bytes`, but the queue visibility resolver only emits `size_bytes`. When the analyst queue bootstrap merges resolved items over persisted Explorer rows, a valid size can be replaced with `0`.
issues.impact: Persisted analyst buckets can come back with incorrect zero-byte metadata even though the original Explorer rows had the right size, which degrades triage confidence and downstream size-based reasoning in the UI.

issues.title: Test bench Entities tab still calls a deprecated route and drops all entity output
issues.summary: `TestOCRPage` loads entities from `/artifacts/{id}/entities` and expects an `entities` array, but that endpoint now just redirects to `/api/graph/artifact/{id}`. The redirected graph payload does not carry `entities`, so the mounted Entities tab resolves to an empty list.
issues.impact: The live demo bench still has a broken Entities tab even when graph/entity data exists for the selected artifact.

issues.title: Demo bench upload lane no longer enforces the PDF-only testcase contract
issues.summary: `testcase.md` defines the demo bench as a PDF upload lane, but the live file input has no `accept` filter and the upload handler posts any selected file into the fixed demo scope.
issues.impact: The bench can accept arbitrary files that do not fit the promised OCR/translation/overlay demo story, which makes the demo surface less deterministic than the documented contract.

issues.title: Demo bench runtime rail still shows configured providers, not the backend actually used
issues.summary: The testcase contract says the bench should show the OCR and translation backend actually used, but `TestOCRPage` derives its runtime chips from current feature flags and provider registry state instead of the selected artifact’s executed OCR/translation path.
issues.impact: The live bench can tell the user “TranslateGemma” or “Surya” is in use even when the current artifact actually ran through a different OCR or translation lane, which makes the runtime status rail misleading.

issues.title: Demo bench `Intelligence Chat` still mis-scopes artifact queries by sending UUID as a file-path filter
issues.summary: When an artifact is selected, `TestOCRPage` scopes chat queries by setting `path=<artifact UUID>` on `/search/unified`. The unified search stack treats `path` as a file-path prefix, not an artifact selector, so the chat tab is not actually grounded to the selected artifact context.
issues.impact: The live chat tab can silently answer from the wrong retrieval scope or from no artifact context at all, even when the UI implies that the selected artifact is constraining the query.

issues.title: Demo bench “Copy current output” still copies the wrong payload on the chat tab
issues.summary: The bench keeps the global copy button visible on all output tabs, but `currentOutputForCopy()` has no `llm` branch. When `Intelligence Chat` is active, copy falls through to `layoutJson` instead of the chat transcript.
issues.impact: Analysts using the live chat tab can think they copied the conversation, but the clipboard receives unrelated layout content or an empty string instead.

issues.title: Demo bench still reads the old artifact-jobs response shape and drops job state
issues.summary: The artifact-jobs endpoint now returns a paginated `items` envelope, but `TestOCRPage` still reads `data.jobs`. As a result, job state in the bench stays empty even when the backend returns jobs.
issues.impact: The live demo bench can underreport queued/running/failed work and make the action surface look idle even while artifact jobs are actually present.

issues.title: Demo bench preview can fail closed after upload because the optimistic artifact carries no `case_id`
issues.summary: The bench seeds an optimistic artifact with only `system_id`, then mounts `PreviewPanel` with only `systemId` in `explorerState`. Under `ENFORCE_CASE_AUTH`, artifact reads fail when no `case_id` is provided, so the just-uploaded artifact can transiently hit 403-style preview/detail failures until the hydrated artifact payload arrives.
issues.impact: Fresh uploads can enter a short broken state in the live bench where preview/detail actions fail even though the artifact was successfully created, especially in stricter scoped deployments.

issues.title: Demo bench Ops drawer still renders a dead “Recent Logs” section
issues.summary: `TestOCRPage` shows a Recent Logs panel, but it never fetches logs. The polling loop hard-resets `opsLogs` to `[]`, and no code populates it from `/ops/logs`.
issues.impact: The live bench advertises recent pipeline logs but always renders “No logs,” which makes the ops drawer partly fake and hides useful troubleshooting context.

issues.title: Scoped Ops intelligence cards still misrender global-only telemetry as subsystem degradation
issues.summary: `ops_monitor` only returns `system` and `knowledge_base` data in global scope, but `OpsDashboard` always renders the search and CIM cards from those fields. In case/bucket scope, missing global-only telemetry is treated as `DEGRADED` search and `INITIALIZING` knowledge graph rather than “not available in scoped view.”
issues.impact: Scoped Ops views can falsely imply that search or CIM health is bad when the real issue is that those cards are consuming global-only telemetry in a scoped page.

issues.title: Scoped Ops page still mounts a global infrastructure-fleet tab
issues.summary: The `Containers` tab is always available inside `OpsDashboard`, but `ContainerManagementPanel` loads Docker containers and live container logs from global endpoints with no scope. The surrounding case/bucket selection does not affect that data at all.
issues.impact: A user navigating a scoped Ops page can still browse deployment-wide infrastructure nodes and raw container logs, which mixes case/bucket operations with global host telemetry and weakens the page’s scope semantics.

issues.title: Analyst workspace preview still drops bucket scope on the mounted preview surface
issues.summary: `AnalystPage` mounts `PreviewPanel` with only `caseId` and `systemId`, while the panel’s scoped actions, detail reads, security actions, overlay reads, and similarity calls rely on `explorerState.bucketId` for bucket scoping.
issues.impact: Bucket-scoped analyst review can still widen inside the live preview workspace, including artifact detail, security, overlay, and related-signal paths.

issues.title: Analyst queue persistence still loses original bucket scope during Explorer-to-queue handoff
issues.summary: Explorer queues raw artifact rows without attaching the active `bucketId`, those row payloads do not include `bucket_id`, and the analyst queue bootstrap later reconciles persisted artifacts with `caseIds` only. Saved analyst queues therefore rehydrate under case scope instead of the original bucket scope.
issues.impact: Analysts can save a bucket-scoped queue and later reopen it with artifacts re-resolved from the whole case, which breaks triage isolation and makes queue state unstable across refreshes.

issues.title: Scoped Ops UI still pauses queued jobs globally
issues.summary: `OpsDashboard` shows a case/bucket scope selector and uses scoped monitor/job queries, but the mounted “pause queued” action posts only `{ purge_broker: true }` to a backend endpoint that pauses every queued job in the system.
issues.impact: An operator looking at a case- or bucket-scoped Ops view can still freeze the global queue, which is a dangerous mismatch between visible scope and destructive control behavior.

issues.title: Scoped Ops logs tab still drops page scope and falls back to the unscoped/default log route
issues.summary: `OpsDashboard` tracks `case_id`, `bucket_id`, and `global_scope` in page state, but it mounts `OpsLogPanel` without passing any scope. `OpsLogPanel` then calls `getOpsLogs(params)` with only local filters like `status`, `job_id`, and `limit`, so the backend never receives the surrounding Ops scope.
issues.impact: The mounted logs tab is inconsistent with the scoped Ops page. In scoped investigations it cannot honor the active case/bucket, and on runtimes that require explicit scope it degrades into request failures instead of showing the expected event stream.

issues.title: Scoped Ops dashboard still exposes global case routing policies
issues.summary: The live Ops overview always mounts `CaseManagementWidget`, which fetches `/ops/case-config` with no scope. The backend route simply returns up to 1,000 cases and their configs regardless of the current case/bucket Ops view.
issues.impact: A user operating inside a scoped Ops page still sees cross-case routing policy data from the whole deployment, which breaks the visible scope model and leaks unrelated case metadata into a local investigation surface.

issues.title: Scoped Ops logs panel still exposes a global log-prune control
issues.summary: The mounted `OpsLogPanel` shows a “Prune Old Logs” button inside the scoped Ops page, but the handler always calls `DELETE /ops/logs?older_than_days=7` with no case/bucket/global scope. The backend prune route is global and deletes matching events across the whole deployment.
issues.impact: An operator who appears to be working inside a case- or bucket-scoped log view can still delete old logs globally, which is a destructive scope/control mismatch on a mounted admin surface.

issues.title: Scoped Ops dashboard still exposes global runtime-flag toggles
issues.summary: The “Model Subsystems” card is mounted inside the same scoped Ops page, but its switches call the global `/ops/feature-flags` endpoint. The page never passes case/bucket scope to those updates, and the backend mutates shared runtime flags rather than any scoped policy object.
issues.impact: An operator standing in what looks like a case- or bucket-scoped Ops surface can still change global OCR/translation/runtime behavior for the whole deployment, which is a dangerous scope/control mismatch on a live mounted admin control.

issues.title: Scoped Ops dashboard still fetches and renders global provider/control-plane state
issues.summary: Even when the page is in case or bucket mode, `OpsDashboard` still loads models, DLQ, runtime flags, runtime policy, provider inventories, and connector registry state from global endpoints with no scope parameter. Those endpoints return deployment-level control-plane data, not scoped telemetry.
issues.impact: A user standing inside a scoped Ops view still receives deployment-wide provider, runtime-policy, and connector state, which breaks the visible scope model and leaks control-plane information unrelated to the active case/bucket.

issues.title: PreviewPanel’s mounted keylogs tab still widens bucket-scoped review to case/system scope
issues.summary: `PreviewPanel` mounts `KeylogsPanel` as a live tab, but the panel never forwards `bucketId` and the backend keylog summary route does not accept it. In bucket-scoped review, the mounted keylog analysis widens to whichever broader case/system scope is available.
issues.impact: Analysts working inside a bucket can still see keylog-derived apps, windows, URLs, and domains from the wider case/system, which breaks review isolation on a mounted preview surface.

issues.title: PreviewPanel passes the wrong IDs into GraphPanel highlighting for similar artifacts

issues.title: `OpsDashboard` only snapshots URL scope once and does not re-sync when router query params change
issues.summary: The mounted Ops surface initializes scope from `window.location.search` once via `useState(getInitialOpsScope)`, then only writes state back to the URL. It never re-reads new query params after navigation.
issues.impact: Scoped deep links or in-app navigations to `/ops?case_id=...&bucket_id=...` can stay stale until a full remount, so the mounted Ops page can show the wrong scope even when the URL changes.

issues.title: `ExplorerPage` only bootstraps from URL query state once and can ignore later scoped navigations
issues.summary: Explorer reads `artifact_id`, case/system/bucket scope, search term, mode, and filters from `window.location.search` only in `getExplorerBootstrapState()`, memoizes that once, and then manages local state separately from React Router query updates.
issues.impact: In-app navigations or deep links to a new `/explorer?...` query can leave the mounted Explorer view showing stale scope, stale search mode, or the wrong selected artifact until the page remounts.

issues.title: Settings still ignores KB deep links because query params do not switch the active menu to `kb`
issues.summary: `SettingsPage` reads `kb_tab`, `kb_q`, and `case_id` from the URL, but `activeMenu` stays initialized to `system` unless the user manually clicks the sidebar.
issues.impact: Links like `/settings?kb_tab=terms&kb_q=...` still land on the system configuration page instead of the KB review surface, so KB review/navigation remains broken in active UI flows.

issues.title: Dashboard does not clear scope when `case_id` or `system_id` is removed from the URL
issues.summary: The dashboard mirrors query params into local state, but it only updates `caseId` and `systemId` when those params are present. Removing them from the URL leaves the previous scope active.
issues.impact: In-app navigations or shared links that intentionally remove dashboard scope can still leave analysts stuck in the previous case/system context until they manually reset it in the UI.

issues.title: The persistent AppShell sidebar still drops all active scope when switching modules
issues.summary: The global navigation shell always links to bare top-level routes like `/explorer`, `/graph`, `/ops`, `/dashboard`, and `/settings` without carrying current case/system/bucket context.
issues.impact: Analysts can establish a scoped review context in one module and then lose it immediately when switching modules through the always-visible shell navigation.

issues.title: Explorer sidebar still loads batches by `system_id` only and ignores the active case scope
issues.summary: The sidebar requests `/explorer/batches?system_id=...` even though the backend batches route also supports `case_id`.
issues.impact: If the same system identity is shared across cases, the Explorer batch picker can widen beyond the active case and show mixed batch history.

issues.title: Explorer sidebar still makes case-only browsing impossible by auto-selecting the first system
issues.summary: As soon as systems load for a selected case, the sidebar forces `systemId` to the first returned system whenever no system is already selected.
issues.impact: The Explorer UI cannot stay at a valid case-wide scope even though the backend list/search routes support `case_id` without `system_id`, so analysts are forced into a narrower system view than they selected.

issues.title: Explorer queue actions still use a page-local `selectedItems` snapshot instead of authoritative selected IDs
issues.summary: Explorer persists `selectedIds` and `selectedItems` separately, but `selectedItems` is only recomputed during explicit selection events from the current visible page. Queueing later uses that stale snapshot directly.
issues.impact: After pagination or filter changes, the queue action can enqueue stale page-local items that no longer match the current `selectedIds`, so analyst triage selection becomes inconsistent and hard to trust.

issues.title: Explorer table view still has a live header/body column mismatch and drops file size from rows
issues.summary: The mounted table header advertises both `Magnitude` and `Timestamp`, but each file row renders only one right-aligned metadata cell with the modified date. The size column is missing in table view.
issues.impact: The active Explorer table surface mislabels columns and hides file-size information that is still shown in gallery mode, which makes file triage inconsistent across the two mounted views.

issues.title: Explorer list pagination still loads and groups the full scoped result set in Python before slicing pages
issues.summary: The backend `/explorer/list` route applies scope filters in SQL, but then materializes all matching artifacts with `_load_explorer_items_with_o2_filters()`, groups folders/files in Python, and only applies `offset`/`limit` after the full combined result is built.
issues.impact: Large Explorer scopes still pay full-scan cost for each page load, so pagination is not actually scalable and will degrade badly on deep or high-volume evidence trees.

issues.title: `DocumentViewer` still carries stale zoom/rotation/pan state across artifact changes
issues.summary: `DocumentViewer` initializes transform state once and exposes a manual reset, but it never resets that state when `src` or `type` changes. Mounted PreviewPanel consumers also do not key the viewer by artifact or source URL.
issues.impact: Switching between images or preview pages can leave the next artifact opened with the previous artifact’s zoom, rotation, and pan offsets, which makes the mounted document-review surface feel broken and disorienting.

issues.title: The blueprint still treats the O2 Tauri/Rust desktop client as part of scope, but the repo is still browser-only
issues.summary: The roadmap still says O2 should include a Tauri + React universal client with Rust owning desktop/backend duties, but the current repo only contains the browser UI package and no Tauri/Rust app scaffold.
issues.impact: The roadmap still overstates desktop-client delivery and ingestion architecture relative to the actual browser-only runtime and repo layout.
issues.notes: The roadmap still says O2 should include a Tauri + React universal client with Rust owning desktop/backend duties, but the current repo only contains the browser UI package and no Tauri/Rust app scaffold.

issues.title: PreviewPanel can carry `htmlPreviewLoading` across artifact changes and block the next Office HTML view
issues.summary: When the selected artifact changes, PreviewPanel clears `htmlPreview` and `htmlPreviewError` but does not clear `htmlPreviewLoading`. The Office HTML auto-load effect then bails out whenever `htmlPreviewLoading` is still true.
issues.impact: Switching artifacts while an Office HTML view is still loading can leave the next Office artifact stuck without auto-loading its HTML preview until the state is manually disturbed.

issues.title: GraphPage does not clear case/bucket/query/mode state when those params are removed from the URL
issues.summary: GraphPage mirrors URL params into local state, but only when those params are present or persisted. Removing them from the URL leaves the previous local state active.
issues.impact: Shared links or in-app navigations that intentionally clear graph scope or query can still leave analysts stuck in the previous graph context until they manually reset it in the UI.

issues.title: GraphPage still keeps stale selected evidence/node context when the query changes
issues.summary: GraphPage resets `selectedNode`, `selectedEvidence`, and `neighborhood` when scope or search mode changes, but not when the query itself changes. New search results can therefore render while the right-hand graph context still shows the previous selection.
issues.impact: Analysts can type a new graph query and still see graph context from the old evidence/node selection, which makes the mounted graph workspace present stale context as if it belongs to the new result set.

issues.title: PreviewPanel leaves stale action success and override/connector errors visible when the selected artifact changes
issues.summary: When the selected artifact changes, PreviewPanel resets the generic `error`, similar/table state, and HTML preview state, but it does not clear `actionSuccess`, `classificationOverrideError`, `securityOverrideError`, or `connectorRunError`. Those states are all rendered in the mounted panel, so the next artifact can inherit success/error banners from the previous artifact.
issues.impact: Switching artifacts after an override, connector run, or other preview action can show the new artifact with stale success/error messaging from the previous artifact, which makes mounted review state misleading and hard to trust.

issues.title: PreviewPanel does not reset override/connector loading flags when the selected artifact changes
issues.summary: The artifact-switch reset block clears most preview state, but it does not clear `classificationOverrideLoading`, `securityOverrideLoading`, or `connectorRunLoading`. Those loading flags directly disable or mark busy the mounted override and connector controls.
issues.impact: If an analyst switches artifacts while an override or connector action is still in flight, the next artifact can open with disabled override buttons or a fake “Connector Running” busy state until the previous request settles.

issues.title: OfficeDocumentWorkspace keeps stale width and zoom state across document changes
issues.summary: `OfficeDocumentWorkspace` initializes `widthPreset` and `zoom` from props once, but it never resets them when `iframeSrc`, `iframeKey`, or the mounted document changes. Both the detached Office page and PreviewPanel reuse the component across artifact changes.
issues.impact: After resizing or zooming one Office document, opening the next document can inherit the previous document’s workspace size and zoom, which makes the mounted Office review flow feel inconsistent and stateful in the wrong way.

issues.title: PreviewPanel can get stranded on a hidden Keylogs tab state
issues.summary: The Keylogs tab is only added when `explorerState.sourceArea === 'Keylogs'`, but PreviewPanel does not reset or normalize `activeTab` when the selected artifact or source area changes. Rendering only handles `activeTab` values `0`, `2`, `3`, and `5`.
issues.impact: If the panel is left on Keylogs and the next selection does not expose that tab, the mounted preview can land in a blank or mismatched viewport state until the user manually switches tabs.
issues.notes: PreviewPanel now resets `activeTab` back to Content when the current artifact is not a keylog, so the mounted view no longer gets stranded on a hidden Keylogs tab state.

issues.title: The detached Office document page drops HTML preview font metadata entirely
issues.summary: The backend `/preview/html` payload returns detected `fonts`, and the shared `OfficeDocumentWorkspace` has a dedicated font strip, but `OfficeDocumentPage` only builds the `/preview/html/document` iframe URL and never fetches or passes the JSON metadata.
issues.impact: The detached Office workspace still loses font visibility that the backend already computes, which weakens reviewer context for legacy-font and script-heavy documents.
issues.notes: OfficeDocumentPage now fetches `/preview/html` metadata, stores `fonts`, and passes them into `OfficeDocumentWorkspace`, so the detached Office view renders the backend font strip.

issues.title: The detached Office document page does not auto-refresh after queuing preview generation
issues.summary: `OfficeDocumentPage` queues `PREVIEW_ONLY` and then performs a single immediate `handleRefresh()`, but it has no polling or follow-up refresh loop while the preview job is actually running asynchronously.
issues.impact: After clicking `Queue Preview`, the detached Office view can stay stuck on the “not ready” empty state until the user manually refreshes, even when preview generation finishes successfully in the background.
issues.notes: OfficeDocumentPage now polls with repeated `handleRefresh()` calls after queuing `PREVIEW_ONLY` until HTML preview is ready or attempts are exhausted.

issues.title: GraphPanel leaves the previous graph visible when a new graph fetch fails
issues.summary: `GraphPanel` replaces `nodes` and `links` only on successful fetches. If the next artifact graph request fails, the catch path just logs the error and leaves the previous graph data mounted.
issues.impact: Switching to an artifact whose graph read fails can still show the prior artifact’s neighborhood as if it belongs to the new selection, which makes the mounted graph view misleading.
issues.notes: GraphPanel now clears `nodes` and `links` before a new fetch and also clears them on failed fetches, so stale prior graph data no longer remains visible.

issues.title: GraphPanel keeps zoom state across artifact changes
issues.summary: `GraphPanel` stores `zoom` locally and only resets it when the user clicks the maximize/reset control. The artifact-change effect reloads graph data but never resets zoom for the next selection.
issues.impact: After zooming one artifact graph, the next artifact can open at the previous zoom level, which makes graph inspection feel sticky and inconsistent across selections.
issues.notes: GraphPanel now resets `zoom` to the default level whenever the selected artifact changes, so graph inspection no longer inherits sticky zoom state.

issues.title: GraphPage still populates the bucket filter from the global bucket list instead of the active case scope
issues.summary: GraphPage always calls the global `getBuckets()` helper and renders every bucket in the selector, even when a case is selected. The bucket API itself is also global and does not accept `case_id`.
issues.impact: The mounted graph workspace can offer buckets from unrelated cases, which makes scoped graph review easier to misconfigure and harder to trust.
issues.notes: GraphPage always calls the global `getBuckets()` helper and renders every bucket in the selector, even when a case is selected. The bucket API itself is also global and does not accept `case_id`.

issues.title: GraphPage evidence-mode selection still collapses multiple chunk hits from the same artifact into one visual selection
issues.summary: Evidence results use a per-hit key that includes `chunk_id` or `path`, but the selected styling still compares only `selectedEvidence.artifact_id === item.artifact_id`.
issues.impact: If graph lexical/semantic search returns multiple distinct hits from the same artifact, selecting one hit makes all hits from that artifact look selected, which collapses chunk-level evidence distinction in the active graph workspace.
issues.notes: Evidence results use a per-hit key that includes `chunk_id` or `path`, but the selected styling still compares only `selectedEvidence.artifact_id === item.artifact_id`.

issues.title: The runtime codebase footprint has ballooned relative to delivered capability and consolidation goals
issues.summary: The current repo has grown to roughly 544 authored files across code, config, docs, scripts, and tests, with about 313 files in the main runtime/product slice (`api/src`, `common`, `worker/src`, `ui/src`, `config`). That would be less concerning if the architecture were consolidated, but the live system still carries both oversized core modules and multiple parallel/legacy surfaces.
issues.impact: The codebase is now paying both costs at once: fragmentation and oversized modules. That makes drift harder to control, slows audits and onboarding, and increases the chance that behavior changes in one surface do not propagate to the others.
issues.notes: The current repo has grown to roughly 544 authored files across code, config, docs, scripts, and tests, with about 313 files in the main runtime/product slice (`api/src`, `common`, `worker/src`, `ui/src`, `config`). T...

issues.title: IngestPanel path mode exposes case UUID and session ID fields that are ignored by the request it actually sends
issues.summary: The mounted `IngestPanel` shows shared `caseId`, `bucketId`, and `sessionId` inputs for both tabs, but the server-path ingest action only posts `case_name`, `batch_date`, `root_path`, and `bucket_id`.
issues.impact: Analysts can fill in case/session context in the mounted ingest panel and reasonably expect it to apply to server-path ingest, but that context is silently dropped, which makes the form misleading and encourages accidental case duplication or session drift.
issues.notes: The mounted `IngestPanel` shows shared `caseId`, `bucketId`, and `sessionId` inputs for both tabs, but the server-path ingest action only posts `case_name`, `batch_date`, `root_path`, and `bucket_id`.

issues.title: ReportEditor keeps write/preview mode when the active report changes
issues.summary: `ReportEditor` stores `viewMode` as local state and never resets it when `content` or the active report changes. `ReportPanel` swaps report content into the same mounted editor instance without keying it by report.
issues.impact: If an analyst leaves one report in Preview mode, the next selected report opens in the same mode instead of resetting to an expected editing view, which makes report switching feel sticky and confusing.
issues.notes: `ReportEditor` stores `viewMode` as local state and never resets it when `content` or the active report changes. `ReportPanel` swaps report content into the same mounted editor instance without keying it by report.

issues.title: The orchestrator API accepts `system_id` but never enforces or uses it
issues.summary: The orchestrator request model includes `system_id`, and live UI callers pass it, but the router only enforces `case_id` and `bucket_id` before dispatching into the service. The provided `system_id` is otherwise ignored.
issues.impact: Mounted UI surfaces imply system-scoped orchestration, but the backend contract currently treats `system_id` as cosmetic metadata, which weakens scope clarity and can hide mismatches between user intent and enforced scope.
issues.notes: The orchestrator request model includes `system_id`, and live UI callers pass it, but the router only enforces `case_id` and `bucket_id` before dispatching into the service. The provided `system_id` is otherwise ignored.

issues.title: Multi-artifact orchestrator requests can partially queue work before a later artifact fails
issues.summary: The orchestrator route processes `artifact_ids` sequentially and immediately accumulates queued jobs. If a later artifact in the same request raises an uncaught scope or HTTP error, the route aborts after earlier artifacts have already been processed.
issues.impact: A single multi-artifact action request can fail at the API level while still having queued work for a subset of artifacts, which makes the operation non-atomic and hard for the UI to reason about or retry safely.

issues.title: The orchestrator response collapses mixed per-artifact outcomes into misleading top-level statuses
issues.summary: The response reducer only returns `blocked` when all artifacts are blocked, and returns `queued` only when any artifact queued or was already queued. Mixed outcomes such as `blocked + ok` fall through to top-level `ok`.
issues.impact: Batch orchestration callers can receive an `ok` response even when some artifacts were blocked, which hides partial failure and makes UI messaging and retries unreliable.
issues.notes: The response reducer only returns `blocked` when all artifacts are blocked, and returns `queued` only when any artifact queued or was already queued. Mixed outcomes such as `blocked + ok` fall through to top-level `ok`.

issues.title: Metadata-only artifacts skip temporal enrichment entirely because `enrich_artifact` returns before year inference
issues.summary: `enrich_artifact` exits immediately when `text` is empty, but the year inference pipeline itself supports metadata-only inference from `modified_ts`, `created_at`, `batch_date`, and path hints.
issues.impact: Artifacts with weak or missing text content never get document-date enrichment from available metadata, which leaves a large class of files under-enriched even when the repo already has a metadata-based fallback path.
issues.notes: `enrich_artifact` exits immediately when `text` is empty, but the year inference pipeline itself supports metadata-only inference from `modified_ts`, `created_at`, `batch_date`, and path hints.

issues.title: Parsed metadata timestamps are discarded from `document_date` unless the source is `ContentDate`
issues.summary: The temporal extractor can return a parsed timestamp from metadata sources such as `modified_ts`, `created_at`, or `batch_date`, but `enrich_artifact` only assigns `artifact.document_date` when the source is exactly `ContentDate`.
issues.impact: Even when metadata date parsing succeeds, the canonical `document_date` field is cleared, which weakens timeline quality and makes metadata-based document dating effectively invisible downstream.
issues.notes: The temporal extractor can return a parsed timestamp from metadata sources such as `modified_ts`, `created_at`, or `batch_date`, but `enrich_artifact` only assigns `artifact.document_date` when the source is exactly `...

issues.title: Preview runtime treats a zero-byte thumbnail file as a valid visual preview
issues.summary: `compute_preview_status()` checks thumbnails with plain `.exists()` instead of the non-empty-file guard used for other preview assets.
issues.impact: A truncated or zero-byte `thumb.png` can make the shared preview state report visual preview availability even though no usable preview asset exists.
issues.notes: `compute_preview_status()` checks thumbnails with plain `.exists()` instead of the non-empty-file guard used for other preview assets.

issues.title: Preview runtime reports `READY` whenever any visual asset exists, even if preview generation is marked failed
issues.summary: `build_preview_snapshot()` promotes status to `READY` as soon as any visual preview asset is present, before considering `meta.json` failure status or accumulated preview errors.
issues.impact: Corrupt, stale, or partially-generated visual assets can still surface as preview `READY`, which overstates preview health and can mask real generation failures from the UI and ops surfaces.
issues.notes: `build_preview_snapshot()` promotes status to `READY` as soon as any visual preview asset is present, before considering `meta.json` failure status or accumulated preview errors.

issues.title: Manifest ingest marks `ImportBatch` as `COMPLETED` before the queued `IMPORT_FILE` jobs have actually processed the files
issues.summary: The `/intake/from_path` manifest stage sets `import_batch.status = "COMPLETED"` as soon as staging and job publication finish, while `processed_files` remains 0 until worker-side `IMPORT_FILE` jobs run later.
issues.impact: Batch status overstates real ingest completion. A batch can read as `COMPLETED` even though none of its staged files have been imported yet, which weakens ops visibility and makes partial/failed follow-on processing harder to spot.
issues.notes: The `/intake/from_path` manifest stage sets `import_batch.status = "COMPLETED"` as soon as staging and job publication finish, while `processed_files` remains 0 until worker-side `IMPORT_FILE` jobs run later.

issues.title: Arabic-script language detection has no deterministic fallback when `langdetect` is missing or weak
issues.summary: `_script_hint()` intentionally returns `None` for Arabic-script text to avoid collapsing Urdu/Persian/Pashto/Arabic into one label, but `detect_language()` then falls back to `hint or "und"` when `langdetect` is unavailable or fails.
issues.impact: In degraded environments or on weak OCR snippets, clearly Arabic-script text can still resolve to `und`, which weakens downstream translation routing, embeddings, and classification on exactly the language families this repo cares about.
issues.notes: `_script_hint()` intentionally returns `None` for Arabic-script text to avoid collapsing Urdu/Persian/Pashto/Arabic into one label, but `detect_language()` then falls back to `hint or "und"` when `langdetect` is unava...

issues.title: Font-hint language fallback is effectively Bengali-only
issues.summary: `infer_lang_from_font_hints()` only recognizes the legacy Bangla font regex and returns `bn`; every other script/font family falls through to `None`.
issues.impact: The repo already depends on font hints to rescue weak OCR/language detection, but that fallback is effectively one-language-only, which leaves Urdu/Arabic/Persian and other script-heavy Office evidence under-supported.
issues.notes: `infer_lang_from_font_hints()` only recognizes the legacy Bangla font regex and returns `bn`; every other script/font family falls through to `None`.

issues.title: Macro scanning disabled is silently treated as “no macros found” in triage
issues.summary: `SecurityProcessor.scan_for_macros()` returns `{has_macros: false, error: "Scanner disabled"}` when `oletools` is unavailable, but the triage flow only records macro results when `has_macros` is true.
issues.impact: In environments where macro scanning is disabled or missing, Office evidence can still flow through triage with no visible indication that macro inspection never actually ran, which weakens security visibility and can create false confidence in the verdict.
issues.notes: `SecurityProcessor.scan_for_macros()` returns `{has_macros: false, error: "Scanner disabled"}` when `oletools` is unavailable, but the triage flow only records macro results when `has_macros` is true.

issues.title: `has_existing_job()` can silently roll back the caller’s whole transaction on a dedupe probe failure
issues.summary: The shared dedupe helper catches any query exception, calls `db.rollback()`, and then returns a soft fallback instead of surfacing the failure.
issues.impact: A transient DB error during a job-existence check can wipe unrelated pending writes in the same session and then continue execution as if nothing serious happened, which makes enqueue paths non-atomic and very hard to reason about.

issues.title: `JobBuilder.enqueue()` can discard unrelated pending work when it hits the active-job race fallback
issues.summary: The shared enqueue wrapper handles `IntegrityError` by rolling back the whole session before re-reading an existing active job.
issues.impact: In routes that enqueue jobs inside a broader transaction, a race on the active-job unique guard can silently discard other pending changes before returning a seemingly-successful job object.

issues.title: `merge_job_payload_hints()` commits immediately inside the shared dedupe helper
issues.summary: When a deduped active job is upgraded with later payload hints, the helper writes the merged payload and calls `db.commit()` directly.
issues.impact: Deduped enqueue paths can commit mid-request from inside a helper, which breaks transaction boundaries and creates partial-persist behavior if the caller later fails.
issues.notes: When a deduped active job is upgraded with later payload hints, the helper writes the merged payload and calls `db.commit()` directly.

issues.title: Hypothesis confidence scoring loads evidence by raw UUID without enforcing the hypothesis case scope
issues.summary: `_get_evidence_confidence()` resolves `artifact`, `chunk`, `claim`, and `event` evidence by primary key only. The hypothesis engine is constructed with a `case_id`, but those evidence lookups never apply it.
issues.impact: If a caller can add a raw evidence UUID to a hypothesis, the confidence calculation can score evidence from another case, which breaks case isolation inside the CIM hypothesis layer.

issues.title: Hypothesis evidence search advertises chunk support, but chunk search is not implemented
issues.summary: The engine’s default evidence types include `"chunk"`, and the confidence scorer understands chunk evidence, but `search_supporting_evidence()` only searches artifacts, claims, and events.
issues.impact: The hypothesis UI/API surface promises chunk evidence search but cannot actually return chunks, which weakens evidence linking exactly where the system should be most granular.
issues.notes: The engine’s default evidence types include `"chunk"`, and the confidence scorer understands chunk evidence, but `search_supporting_evidence()` only searches artifacts, claims, and events.

issues.title: Event builder still truncates multi-party evidence down to two actors and ten candidate IDs
issues.summary: `process_event_build()` stops after the first two actor candidates and only preserves the first ten entity-candidate IDs in event/claim evidence metadata.
issues.impact: Multi-party communications, meetings, and transactions are flattened into at most two actors with truncated candidate lineage, which makes CIM events and derived hypotheses materially lossy.
issues.notes: `process_event_build()` stops after the first two actor candidates and only preserves the first ten entity-candidate IDs in event/claim evidence metadata.

issues.title: Fused-knowledge source links still drop case and bucket scope
issues.summary: `_artifact_links()` builds bare `/api/artifacts/{id}` preview/layout URLs, and `get_fused_knowledge_view()` returns those links directly even when the fused topic itself is case/bucket scoped.
issues.impact: Fused-knowledge results hand back artifact links that widen out of the active scope, so downstream consumers can drift from the scoped analytical context even when the topic lookup was properly narrowed.
issues.notes: `_artifact_links()` builds bare `/api/artifacts/{id}` preview/layout URLs, and `get_fused_knowledge_view()` returns those links directly even when the fused topic itself is case/bucket scoped.

issues.title: Outbox escalation still checks for a nonexistent `COMPLETED` job state instead of `DONE`
issues.summary: `escalate_exhausted_entries()` excludes `"COMPLETED"` from quarantine escalation, but the canonical job lifecycle uses `DONE`.
issues.impact: The outbox recovery path is comparing against the wrong terminal-state name, which makes job-state handling drift from the canonical enum and risks misclassifying already-finished jobs in exhausted-publish scenarios.
issues.notes: `escalate_exhausted_entries()` excludes `"COMPLETED"` from quarantine escalation, but the canonical job lifecycle uses `DONE`.

issues.title: `db:report_generated` output validation still passes vacuously when `report_id` is missing
issues.summary: The `REPORT_GENERATE` contract declares `payload:report_id` as required and `db:report_generated` as its produced output, but the validator returns `True` for `db:report_generated` whenever `report_id` is absent.
issues.impact: Report jobs with malformed or incomplete payloads can still appear to satisfy output validation, which weakens contract enforcement on an already-fragile reporting path.
issues.notes: The `REPORT_GENERATE` contract declares `payload:report_id` as required and `db:report_generated` as its produced output, but the validator returns `True` for `db:report_generated` whenever `report_id` is absent.

issues.title: Generic translation skip validation is out of sync with the repo’s actual language codes
issues.summary: `validate_skip_conditions()` only treats `lang == "eng"` or empty as skippable for `language_check`, but the canonical language pipeline uses lowercase ISO-639-1 codes like `en` and `und`.
issues.impact: Any generic caller that trusts contract skip-validation instead of the translation-specific helpers can make the wrong skip decision for English and unknown-language artifacts, which keeps contract logic out of sync with runtime behavior.
issues.notes: `validate_skip_conditions()` only treats `lang == "eng"` or empty as skippable for `language_check`, but the canonical language pipeline uses lowercase ISO-639-1 codes like `en` and `und`.

issues.title: Standardized `JobEvent` metadata still drops bucket scope
issues.summary: Job creation augments payloads with `bucket_id` and `bucket_scope`, and the outbox even preserves that scope payload, but `build_event_metadata()` only resolves `case_id` and never includes bucket scope unless each caller adds it manually through `extra`.
issues.impact: The shared audit trail for jobs loses bucket context by default, which makes bucket-scoped investigations harder to trace and weakens observability exactly where scope fidelity matters most.
issues.notes: Job creation augments payloads with `bucket_id` and `bucket_scope`, and the outbox even preserves that scope payload, but `build_event_metadata()` only resolves `case_id` and never includes bucket scope unless each ca...

issues.title: Translation contract skip validation still uses the wrong English code
