issues.summary: The generic `language_check` skip validator only treats `eng` or empty as English/undetected, while the repo’s language detection and translation normalization use canonical lowercase codes like `en` and `und`.
issues.impact: Contract-level skip decisions can drift from real translation routing, so any generic caller relying on `validate_skip_conditions()` can make the wrong decision for English or unknown-language artifacts.
issues.notes: The generic `language_check` skip validator only treats `eng` or empty as English/undetected, while the repo’s language detection and translation normalization use canonical lowercase codes like `en` and `und`.

issues.title: Worker `JOB_RUNNING` events are emitted before bucket scope is augmented into the payload
issues.summary: The dispatcher writes the initial `RUNNING` event using `job.payload`, then augments bucket scope into the local payload only afterward when it builds `JobContext`.
issues.impact: Even when later worker logic runs with bucket-aware context, the initial lifecycle event is recorded without that scope, which weakens auditability for bucket-scoped runs.
issues.notes: The dispatcher writes the initial `RUNNING` event using `job.payload`, then augments bucket scope into the local payload only afterward when it builds `JobContext`.

issues.title: TOY_MODE polling is queue-blind and can claim jobs for unsupported worker roles
issues.summary: In TOY_MODE the worker bypasses `worker_loop()` queue selection and enters `polling_loop()`, which just claims the highest-priority `QUEUED` job globally without filtering by worker role or queue assignment.
issues.impact: A specialized OCR, translate, or base worker running in TOY_MODE can pick up the wrong job classes, which makes local/dev execution diverge from real queue semantics and can hide role-specific failures.
issues.notes: In TOY_MODE the worker bypasses `worker_loop()` queue selection and enters `polling_loop()`, which just claims the highest-priority `QUEUED` job globally without filtering by worker role or queue assignment.

issues.title: Preview and OCR handler wrappers commit “complete” events before the dispatcher has actually finished the job
issues.summary: `handle_preview()` and `handle_ocr()` both commit a terminal-sounding INFO event (`"Preview generation complete"` / `"OCR processing complete"`) before dispatcher output validation, DONE transition, and final commit have succeeded.
issues.impact: If validation or follow-on bookkeeping fails after those commits, the job can end `FAILED` while its event stream already claims the operation completed, which creates contradictory audit history and partial-persist behavior inside the runtime wrapper that was supposed to unify lifecycle semantics.
issues.notes: `handle_preview()` and `handle_ocr()` both commit a terminal-sounding INFO event (`"Preview generation complete"` / `"OCR processing complete"`) before dispatcher output validation, DONE transition, and final commit h...

issues.title: `enrich_artifact()` still commits partial state multiple times mid-job
issues.summary: The enrichment helper commits language detection, document-date inference, and content-simhash updates in separate transactions before later enrichment steps run, and `threat_matcher.scan()` is still unguarded after those commits.
issues.impact: If threat matching or later enrichment fails, the job can still end failed after earlier metadata has already been committed, which breaks the dispatcher’s unified lifecycle semantics and leaves partial artifact state behind.
issues.notes: The enrichment helper commits language detection, document-date inference, and content-simhash updates in separate transactions before later enrichment steps run, and `threat_matcher.scan()` is still unguarded after t...

issues.title: Image-intelligence records a contradictory pipeline mode
issues.summary: `process_image_intelligence()` stores `image_pipeline.mode` from `artifact.source_area` while separately recording `last_run` from the actual `intensity` argument.
issues.impact: A screenshot processed in light mode is still recorded as `mode=heavy`, and a non-screenshot forced through heavy mode is still recorded as `mode=light`, which makes the runtime telemetry and triage state internally contradictory.
issues.notes: `process_image_intelligence()` stores `image_pipeline.mode` from `artifact.source_area` while separately recording `last_run` from the actual `intensity` argument.

issues.title: Reliability drift reporting omits queues that have RabbitMQ messages but no matching DB-queued jobs
issues.summary: `get_reliability_snapshot()` only builds `queue_drift` entries by iterating `db_queued` queues, so it never reports queues that contain broker-side orphan messages with zero corresponding DB rows.
issues.impact: The runtime snapshot can underreport or completely hide broker-only queue drift, which weakens diagnostics for exactly the “messages exist but DB says nothing is queued” failure mode.
issues.notes: `get_reliability_snapshot()` only builds `queue_drift` entries by iterating `db_queued` queues, so it never reports queues that contain broker-side orphan messages with zero corresponding DB rows.

issues.title: Retry republish resets jobs to `QUEUED` without clearing stale worker ownership metadata
issues.summary: On successful retry republish, the consumer only resets `status` and `started_at` before committing. It leaves `worker_id` and `heartbeat_at` from the failed RUNNING attempt intact.
issues.impact: Requeued jobs can still look like they belong to the old worker instance, which pollutes runtime observability and can confuse stale-job recovery or operator debugging.
issues.notes: On successful retry republish, the consumer only resets `status` and `started_at` before committing. It leaves `worker_id` and `heartbeat_at` from the failed RUNNING attempt intact.

issues.title: `JobContext.heartbeat()` silently swallows heartbeat update failures
issues.summary: The worker heartbeat helper logs DB ping failures and completely suppresses callback exceptions instead of failing or surfacing degraded liveness.
issues.impact: Long-running jobs can continue processing even while heartbeat updates are failing, which increases the risk of stale-job recovery reclaiming work that is still actively running and creates duplicate-processing hazards.
issues.notes: The worker heartbeat helper logs DB ping failures and completely suppresses callback exceptions instead of failing or surfacing degraded liveness.

issues.title: Stale RUNNING recovery can requeue jobs without creating a deliverable outbox entry
issues.summary: `recover_stale_jobs()` resets a stale RUNNING job to `QUEUED` and clears `published_at`, but `_ensure_outbox_entry()` can still refuse to create a new outbox row when it sees a recent published delivery record.
issues.impact: A recovered stale job can end up back in `QUEUED` with no unpublished outbox row, which leaves it stranded until a later recovery sweep notices it again instead of making it immediately deliverable.
issues.notes: `recover_stale_jobs()` resets a stale RUNNING job to `QUEUED` and clears `published_at`, but `_ensure_outbox_entry()` can still refuse to create a new outbox row when it sees a recent published delivery record.

issues.title: Process tracking links threads by name without scoping by case
issues.summary: `ProcessTrackingProcessor.link_event_to_thread()` looks up `ProcessThread` by `name` only and does not include `case_id` in the lookup.
issues.impact: Process threads can merge across cases when names collide, and the daily auto-thread fallback is effectively global, which breaks case isolation in the process-tracking layer.

issues.title: Process tracking commits inside the processor, outside dispatcher lifecycle control
issues.summary: `ProcessTrackingProcessor.link_event_to_thread()` commits its own DB transaction while running as an enrichment follow-on.
issues.impact: Process-tracking writes can persist even if the enclosing job later fails, which creates the same partial-persist/lifecycle drift already seen in other worker helpers.
issues.notes: `ProcessTrackingProcessor.link_event_to_thread()` commits its own DB transaction while running as an enrichment follow-on.

issues.title: Worker-side KB hit extraction still ignores bucket scope
issues.summary: `apply_knowledge_hits()` reads KB terms with only `case_id`, even though the shared KB scope helper supports bucket-aware narrowing.
issues.impact: Bucket-scoped analyst KB terms are ignored during worker-side enrichment, and case-scoped term matches can still widen past the active bucket.

issues.title: Worker-side KB helpers still commit mid-enrichment
issues.summary: `apply_knowledge_hits()` and `extract_alias_candidates()` both call `db.commit()` internally instead of leaving transaction control to the caller.
issues.impact: KB hits and alias candidates can persist even if later steps in the same worker job fail, which creates more partial-state drift inside the enrichment pipeline.
issues.notes: `apply_knowledge_hits()` and `extract_alias_candidates()` both call `db.commit()` internally instead of leaving transaction control to the caller.

issues.title: `o1_o2_full` only queues a partial pipeline on the common paths
issues.summary: `_enqueue_full_pipeline_jobs()` returns immediately after queuing OCR, text extraction, or translation, so embedding, NER, and deep analysis are never queued for the common “needs OCR / needs text / needs translate” cases.
issues.impact: The action is named and messaged like a full O1/O2 pipeline trigger, but for many artifacts it only seeds the first missing stage and requires manual re-runs to reach later analysis stages.
issues.notes: `_enqueue_full_pipeline_jobs()` returns immediately after queuing OCR, text extraction, or translation, so embedding, NER, and deep analysis are never queued for the common “needs OCR / needs text / needs translate” c...

issues.title: `ocr_force` deletes OCR/translation state before it knows rerun enqueue succeeded
issues.summary: The force-OCR path clears derived files, deletes OCR/translated chunks, nulls `has_ocr`, and commits those destructive changes before it calls `enqueue_action()` for the replacement OCR job.
issues.impact: If enqueue fails after the cleanup commit, the artifact is left with OCR/translation state wiped and no replacement OCR job queued, which is a destructive partial-failure path.

issues.title: `artifact_state` overstates raw/OCR/translated layer presence
issues.summary: `_build_text_layers_state()` treats `ResolvedArtifactText.text is not None` as presence, but the resolver returns `text=""` on empty fallback. It also uses `ocr_preferred` and `translated` fallback chains, so raw text can make `has_ocr` or `has_translated` appear true.
issues.impact: Runtime state payloads can claim OCR or translated text layers exist when only fallback raw text exists, or even when the resolved text is empty, which misleads mounted diagnostics and pipeline recommendations.
issues.notes: `_build_text_layers_state()` treats `ResolvedArtifactText.text is not None` as presence, but the resolver returns `text=""` on empty fallback. It also uses `ocr_preferred` and `translated` fallback chains, so raw text...

issues.title: Generic forced reruns fail active jobs before replacement enqueue succeeds
issues.summary: `enqueue_action(..., force_new=True)` marks matching `QUEUED` and `RUNNING` jobs as `FAILED` and commits that change before it calls the deduped enqueue helper for the replacement job.
issues.impact: A forced translate/analysis rerun can kill the currently active job and then fail before the replacement is queued, leaving the artifact with no live job at all. This is the same destructive partial-failure shape already seen in `ocr_force`, but it exists in the shared action helper for every `force_new` caller.

issues.title: Text-dependent actions persist triage mutations before enqueue succeeds
issues.summary: `handle_text_dependent_action()` writes and commits `triage_update` metadata before it calls `enqueue_action()` for the actual translation/NER job.
issues.impact: Flags such as `ner_after_translate` can be persisted even when the downstream job never gets queued, which makes artifact state and operator expectations drift away from actual pipeline execution.
issues.notes: `handle_text_dependent_action()` writes and commits `triage_update` metadata before it calls `enqueue_action()` for the actual translation/NER job.

issues.title: Translation readiness still accepts empty marker files as successful output
issues.summary: The shared translation-state helpers treat `translation.md`, `translated.md`, `translation.txt`, `translated.txt`, and `translated_layout.json` as ready based on `.exists()` alone, and the canonical readiness helper depends on those checks.
issues.impact: Zero-byte or truncated translation marker files can make the runtime believe translation is complete, which blocks reruns and misreports pipeline readiness to mounted preview/detail surfaces.
issues.notes: The shared translation-state helpers treat `translation.md`, `translated.md`, `translation.txt`, `translated.txt`, and `translated_layout.json` as ready based on `.exists()` alone, and the canonical readiness helper d...

issues.title: Translation defaults are internally contradictory across runtime policy and worker execution
issues.summary: The shared defaults declare `DEFAULT_TRANSLATE_ENGINE = "translategemma"`, but the same module also advertises `SUPPORTED_TRANSLATION_DEFAULT = "nllb"` and excludes `translategemma` from `SUPPORTED_TRANSLATION_ENGINES`. Ops returns both values, while the worker actually resolves its default engine from `DEFAULT_TRANSLATE_ENGINE`.
issues.impact: Runtime-policy surfaces can report one supported default while the worker silently executes another engine by default, which creates operator confusion and makes translation behavior harder to reason about or validate.
issues.notes: The shared defaults declare `DEFAULT_TRANSLATE_ENGINE = "translategemma"`, but the same module also advertises `SUPPORTED_TRANSLATION_DEFAULT = "nllb"` and excludes `translategemma` from `SUPPORTED_TRANSLATION_ENGINES...

issues.title: Translation-family readiness can falsely mark TranslateGemma healthy when another engine is selected
issues.summary: The TranslateGemma readiness probe sets `available=True` whenever the selected engine is not TranslateGemma and hardcodes the note `"TranslateGemma not selected; IndicTrans2 active"`, even when the selected engine is actually `nllb` or `marian`.
issues.impact: Ops/model-readiness surfaces can show a healthy TranslateGemma lane even when that engine is not selected or not relevant, and they can mislabel the active fallback family entirely. That makes translation readiness telemetry less trustworthy during real incidents.
issues.notes: The TranslateGemma readiness probe sets `available=True` whenever the selected engine is not TranslateGemma and hardcodes the note `"TranslateGemma not selected; IndicTrans2 active"`, even when the selected engine is ...

issues.title: Ingest-event duplicate accounting is internally inconsistent
issues.summary: `create_ingest_event()` has a duplicate-upload counter branch, but `duplicate` is excluded from `counted_statuses`, so that branch can never run and duplicate statuses would not increment `total_files`.
issues.impact: Duplicate-file session accounting is internally inconsistent. If duplicate event statuses are emitted or reintroduced, ingest-session totals and uploaded-file counts will silently drift.
issues.notes: `create_ingest_event()` has a duplicate-upload counter branch, but `duplicate` is excluded from `counted_statuses`, so that branch can never run and duplicate statuses would not increment `total_files`.

issues.title: Empty OCR output can still advertise translation input readiness
issues.summary: `persist_empty_ocr_result()` writes an empty `ocr.txt` and materializes `layout.json` as `[]`, while pipeline-state logic later treats any existing `layout.json` as enough to set `has_translation_input=True`.
issues.impact: An artifact with explicitly empty OCR output can still look translation-ready to mounted pipeline-state consumers, which encourages downstream translation attempts even though there is no usable OCR text.
issues.notes: `persist_empty_ocr_result()` writes an empty `ocr.txt` and materializes `layout.json` as `[]`, while pipeline-state logic later treats any existing `layout.json` as enough to set `has_translation_input=True`.

issues.title: Retrieval-layer chunking silently drops text beyond the first 500 retrieval chunks
issues.summary: `add_chunk()` regenerates retrieval chunks and then slices them to `RETRIEVAL_RANGE_SIZE`, discarding any remaining retrieval windows without recording truncation.
issues.impact: Very large raw/OCR/translated documents lose tail retrieval coverage silently, which degrades semantic search and evidence recall for long artifacts while leaving no explicit signal that indexing was truncated.
issues.notes: `add_chunk()` regenerates retrieval chunks and then slices them to `RETRIEVAL_RANGE_SIZE`, discarding any remaining retrieval windows without recording truncation.

issues.title: NER persists completion state before the follow-on event-build chain is secured
issues.summary: `process_ner()` commits `ner_done`, `ner_source`, and triage metadata inside `_mark_ner_done()`, while the runtime handler only tries to enqueue `EVENT_BUILD` afterward.
issues.impact: If the follow-on `EVENT_BUILD` enqueue fails, the artifact is still permanently marked as NER-complete. That creates another partial-state path where upstream analysis looks finished but downstream semantic extraction never actually ran.
issues.notes: `process_ner()` commits `ner_done`, `ner_source`, and triage metadata inside `_mark_ner_done()`, while the runtime handler only tries to enqueue `EVENT_BUILD` afterward.

issues.title: Hypothesis mutation/status routes are still raw-UUID scoped instead of case scoped
issues.summary: The CIM hypothesis endpoints instantiate `HypothesisEngine` with a case, but both the router and the engine still load hypotheses by `hypothesis_id` alone for add/remove/search/status operations.
issues.impact: Anyone who knows a hypothesis UUID can still mutate evidence, search context, recalculate confidence, or update status across case boundaries. The code looks case-scoped, but the actual enforcement is missing on the critical read/write path.

issues.title: Duplicate artifact selections can break report queueing or create duplicate report links
issues.summary: `queue_report_generation()` iterates the raw `artifact_ids` list directly when building `AnalysisBundleItem` and `ReportArtifact` rows. Duplicate IDs are not normalized away first.
issues.impact: A duplicate artifact in the selection payload can trigger a unique-constraint failure on `analysis_bundle_items` or create duplicate `report_artifacts` rows for the same report. That makes report generation sensitive to client-side duplication and can fail an otherwise valid report request.
issues.notes: `queue_report_generation()` iterates the raw `artifact_ids` list directly when building `AnalysisBundleItem` and `ReportArtifact` rows. Duplicate IDs are not normalized away first.

issues.title: `TEXT_EXTRACT` commits handoff and ready state before follow-on jobs are secured
issues.summary: `process_text_extract()` commits triage/text-layer updates before it enqueues the next-stage job in all three main branches: OCR handoff for unicode recovery, OCR handoff for normal fallback, and `INDEX_NOW` after successful text extraction.
issues.impact: If the downstream enqueue fails, the artifact can remain marked as `ocr_handoff` or `text_ready`, with chunks and triage already persisted, but no `OCR_QUICK` or `INDEX_NOW` job actually scheduled. That leaves the pipeline in a misleading partial state.
issues.notes: `process_text_extract()` commits triage/text-layer updates before it enqueues the next-stage job in all three main branches: OCR handoff for unicode recovery, OCR handoff for normal fallback, and `INDEX_NOW` after suc...

issues.title: Worker still queues NER jobs even when resolved text is empty
issues.summary: `queue_ner_job()` resolves artifact text and computes a signature, but it never checks whether any text was actually found before creating the `NER` job.
issues.impact: Empty-text artifacts still consume NER job slots and DB work only to be marked `empty` later. This is wasted pipeline load and contributes to the repo’s already-slow NLP path.
issues.notes: `queue_ner_job()` resolves artifact text and computes a signature, but it never checks whether any text was actually found before creating the `NER` job.

issues.title: Cross-case duplicate intake can merge artifacts into the same evidence family
issues.summary: Duplicate intake reuses the canonical artifact from any case, then attaches the new duplicate artifact to that anchor's evidence family with no case-boundary check.
issues.impact: The same document hash imported into different cases can end up sharing one `EvidenceFamily`, which breaks case isolation in lineage and downstream semantic grouping. The duplicate metadata is intentionally cross-case aware, but the durable family assignment should not silently collapse separate cases into one lineage family.

issues.title: Canonical resolver loses scoped priority for bucket/case manual overrides
issues.summary: The resolver loads scoped `KnowledgeTerm` entries through `apply_knowledge_term_resolution_scope()`, but then records every manual override with `scope="global"`. Collision handling can no longer prefer bucket/case-scoped manual overrides over global ones.
issues.impact: When a global knowledge term and a bucket/case-scoped knowledge term share the same label or alias, canonical resolution can keep the wrong one based on iteration order instead of scoped intent. That weakens bucket-aware entity/search resolution exactly where scoped analyst overrides are supposed to win.
issues.notes: The resolver loads scoped `KnowledgeTerm` entries through `apply_knowledge_term_resolution_scope()`, but then records every manual override with `scope="global"`. Collision handling can no longer prefer bucket/case-sc...

issues.title: Canonical-entity search can backfill the wrong graph node outside scope
issues.summary: When a `CanonicalEntity` lacks `node_id`, `search_canonical_entities()` falls back to `Node` lookup by label and type only, without applying case or bucket scope.
issues.impact: A scoped graph/entity search can return a `node_id` from another case’s graph when labels collide, and the mounted graph page will navigate/highlight that node directly. This is a correctness leak even when the canonical entity row itself was otherwise in-scope.
issues.notes: When a `CanonicalEntity` lacks `node_id`, `search_canonical_entities()` falls back to `Node` lookup by label and type only, without applying case or bucket scope.

issues.title: Entity-profile lookup also ignores scoped KB precedence on collisions
issues.summary: `lookup_entity_profile()` resolves matching `KnowledgeTerm` rows through bucket/case-aware scope, but then chooses the winning term by match score only. When multiple rows tie on score, scoped terms do not outrank global ones.
issues.impact: A bucket/case-scoped knowledge term can lose to a global term with the same label or alias, which means the entity profile shown to analysts may carry the wrong severity, notes, related terms, and reference links for the active scope.
issues.notes: `lookup_entity_profile()` resolves matching `KnowledgeTerm` rows through bucket/case-aware scope, but then chooses the winning term by match score only. When multiple rows tie on score, scoped terms do not outrank glo...

issues.title: Shared job-contract file validation still treats zero-byte outputs as success
issues.summary: `job_validation._check_requirement()` considers any declared `files:*` output satisfied if the path merely exists. It does not enforce non-empty content, even though many contracts use `files:*` to prove OCR, preview, translation, and face-detection outputs.
issues.impact: A worker can mark `OCR_*`, `TRANSLATE`, `PREVIEW_GENERATE`, `THUMB_PHASH`, or `FACE_DETECT` outputs valid when the produced file is empty or corrupt-but-present. This is the same class of bug already seen in preview/translation readiness, but here it exists at the shared contract-validation layer, so it can silently bless bad outputs across multiple pipelines.
issues.notes: `job_validation._check_requirement()` considers any declared `files:*` output satisfied if the path merely exists. It does not enforce non-empty content, even though many contracts use `files:*` to prove OCR, preview,...

issues.title: Shared text resolution is still lossy and single-slice only
issues.summary: `common.text_resolution.resolve_artifact_text()` returns the first non-empty preferred file or one preferred chunk among `200`, `1`, and `0`, truncated to `max_chars`. It never assembles retrieval-layer chunks or multiple chunk windows, even though many downstream processors treat it as the canonical “best text” view.
issues.impact: Reports, embeddings, image-intel hints, artifact-state summaries, and other consumers can operate on a narrow first-slice of the document rather than the actual best available text corpus. Long documents and retrieval-rich artifacts therefore look emptier or less informative than they really are, which contributes directly to weak summaries, slow/ineffective embeddings, and misleading layer-state telemetry.
issues.notes: `common.text_resolution.resolve_artifact_text()` returns the first non-empty preferred file or one preferred chunk among `200`, `1`, and `0`, truncated to `max_chars`. It never assembles retrieval-layer chunks or mult...

issues.title: Shared LLM context still ignores its `max_chunks` contract entirely
issues.summary: `resolve_artifact_context()` and `read_artifact_context()` expose a `max_chunks` parameter, and multiple callers pass `max_chunks=5`, but the helper ignores it and simply delegates to `resolve_artifact_text()` with `max_chars` only.
issues.impact: Deep analysis and analyst-report generation appear to be bounded by chunk count, but in reality they are still fed one lossy first-slice of text. The API contract is misleading, and tuning `max_chunks` has no effect on context quality or runtime cost.
issues.notes: `resolve_artifact_context()` and `read_artifact_context()` expose a `max_chunks` parameter, and multiple callers pass `max_chunks=5`, but the helper ignores it and simply delegates to `resolve_artifact_text()` with `m...

issues.title: Search fallback summaries still collapse distinct artifacts by file path alone
issues.summary: `fallback_summary()` dedupes both the top-path list and rendered evidence lines by `canonical_path(path)` only. It does not include `artifact_id`, `system_id`, or case scope in the dedupe key, even though the router uses it to summarize multi-artifact evidence sets.
issues.impact: If multiple artifacts share the same path string across systems, cases, or duplicate imports, the fallback answer can collapse them into one “top evidence” line. That underreports evidence breadth and can make cross-system/cross-batch hits look like one artifact.
issues.notes: `fallback_summary()` dedupes both the top-path list and rendered evidence lines by `canonical_path(path)` only. It does not include `artifact_id`, `system_id`, or case scope in the dedupe key, even though the router u...

issues.title: KB review routes still expose candidate review and canonical listings without case scope
issues.summary: The KB review API lists entity candidates, alias candidates, and canonical entities without requiring case scope, and the review actions mutate candidates by raw UUID only. The service layer honors case scope when provided, but the mounted routes do not require it.
issues.impact: Candidate review and canonical-KB administration still behave like global admin surfaces. An operator can enumerate or review candidates across cases, and canonical listings are not constrained to the active case even though the underlying data model is case-aware.

issues.title: KB review still reuses graph nodes globally across cases
issues.summary: `_ensure_node()` materializes or reuses `Node` rows by `label + type` only. It ignores case scope, and review-time canonical entity materialization uses that helper for both new entities and backfilled `node_id`s.
issues.impact: Two cases that review the same label/type combination can silently attach their canonical entities to the same graph node. That undermines case isolation in the graph layer and can make curated KB review bleed into the wrong case graph.
issues.notes: `_ensure_node()` materializes or reuses `Node` rows by `label + type` only. It ignores case scope, and review-time canonical entity materialization uses that helper for both new entities and backfilled `node_id`s.

issues.title: Search audit helper still commits inside the shared request path
issues.summary: `record_search_event()` performs its own `db.commit()` and `db.rollback()` inside the shared search helper, even though it is called from the main search routes using the request session.
issues.impact: The audit path can still prematurely commit any incidental pending state on the shared request session, and on failure it rolls back the caller’s session out from under the route. Search is mostly read-only today, so this is more transaction hygiene drift than an immediate exploit, but it is still the wrong lifecycle boundary for a shared helper.
issues.notes: `record_search_event()` performs its own `db.commit()` and `db.rollback()` inside the shared search helper, even though it is called from the main search routes using the request session.

issues.title: Model-cache readiness still treats `config.json exists` as a healthy model
issues.summary: `cache_has_model()` marks a local model cached as soon as it finds `config.json`. It does not require non-empty files or any weights/artifact completeness, but readiness snapshots use it to report NER, embeddings, translation, and summarization availability.
issues.impact: Ops/model readiness can still show local models as available when the cache is only partially present or corrupted. This is the same false-positive pattern already present in preview and translation readiness, but here it affects the deployment health surface itself.
issues.notes: `cache_has_model()` marks a local model cached as soon as it finds `config.json`. It does not require non-empty files or any weights/artifact completeness, but readiness snapshots use it to report NER, embeddings, tra...

issues.title: Manual KB canonical creation still omits graph-node materialization
issues.summary: `POST /kb/canonical` creates a `CanonicalEntity` directly and commits it without assigning `node_id`. The review path uses `_ensure_node()`, but the direct create path does not.
issues.impact: Manually created canonical entities start life without a graph node and later rely on the unscoped node backfill path in entity search. That makes the direct admin/create path inconsistent with the review path and amplifies the wrong-node drift already present in graph/entity lookup.
issues.notes: `POST /kb/canonical` creates a `CanonicalEntity` directly and commits it without assigning `node_id`. The review path uses `_ensure_node()`, but the direct create path does not.

issues.title: Explorer search result shaping still overwrites real `source_area` with the request filter
issues.summary: `shape_search_results_for_explorer()` writes `source_area=source_area` from the request parameters instead of using the artifact’s actual source area. When no filter is supplied, shaped search results can carry `None` even if the artifact really belongs to a concrete source area.
issues.impact: Explorer search results can lose or falsify source-area identity, which in turn breaks mounted UI behavior that depends on source area semantics. It also helps explain why downstream surfaces like PreviewPanel and analyst handoff still behave inconsistently around keylogs and other source-area-specific features.
issues.notes: `shape_search_results_for_explorer()` writes `source_area=source_area` from the request parameters instead of using the artifact’s actual source area. When no filter is supplied, shaped search results can carry `None`...

issues.title: Classification state still reports legacy/manual fallback values as `detected`
issues.summary: `build_classification_state()` marks `final.source` as `"detected"` whenever there is no explicit override, even though `effective_classification_level()` and `effective_classification_label()` can fall back to legacy `classification_*` fields rather than `content_classification_*`.
issues.impact: The API can present a final classification as if it came from the new content-classifier pipeline when it actually came from legacy/manual `classification_level` or `classification_label` fields. That makes analyst-visible provenance for classification state unreliable even before any explicit override is applied.
issues.notes: `build_classification_state()` marks `final.source` as `"detected"` whenever there is no explicit override, even though `effective_classification_level()` and `effective_classification_label()` can fall back to legacy...

issues.title: Case-scoped search still leaks internal `ProcessedData` through OSINT supporting knowledge
issues.summary: `search_osint()` queries `artifacts + chunks` for OSINT-like rows from any artifact with `source_area='ProcessedData'` or matching source/url hints, but it accepts no `case_id` or `bucket_id`. The case-scoped `/search/unified` overview path calls it unscoped and appends those results as “supporting knowledge”.
issues.impact: A scoped search can surface supporting-knowledge snippets sourced from other cases’ internal processed artifacts, not just public OSINT documents. That is a genuine cross-case leakage path disguised as “external knowledge”.

issues.title: Translation variants and payload reads still treat mere file existence as usable translation
issues.summary: `_build_translation_variants()` marks an engine variant available if any variant file merely exists, and `_read_translation_payload()` returns translated markdown/text or parsed layout whenever those files exist. There is no non-empty/content-validity check on these read paths.
issues.impact: The artifact detail API can advertise a translation engine variant as available when the stored files are empty or corrupt, and the translation read route can return empty content or throw when a stale/invalid `translated_layout.json` exists. This is the same readiness-by-existence bug class already present elsewhere, but here it directly breaks mounted translation UI flows.
issues.notes: `_build_translation_variants()` marks an engine variant available if any variant file merely exists, and `_read_translation_payload()` returns translated markdown/text or parsed layout whenever those files exist. Ther...

issues.title: OpenAI-compatible model probes still report healthy on empty `/models` payloads
issues.summary: `list_models_urllib()` and `probe_models_requests()` return `available=True` on any successful `/models` response, even if `extract_model_ids()` returns an empty list. Downstream readiness helpers then treat that as a healthy remote endpoint and can keep the requested model as the effective model even when the runtime advertised none.
issues.impact: Remote LLM or translation readiness can still show as available when the endpoint answered but advertised no models at all. That is a false-healthy probe path for OpenAI-compatible backends and can silently preserve a requested model that the runtime never exposed.
issues.notes: `list_models_urllib()` and `probe_models_requests()` return `available=True` on any successful `/models` response, even if `extract_model_ids()` returns an empty list. Downstream readiness helpers then treat that as a...

issues.title: Native vision runtime probing still trusts generic JSON health responses as service availability
issues.summary: `probe_native_vision_runtime()` marks a runtime `available=True` as soon as `/vision/runtime` or `/healthz` returns any JSON object. It does not require a healthy status, a matching service entry, or any service-specific readiness marker before returning success.
issues.impact: Surya/Qwen native-vision readiness can still be reported as healthy when the endpoint is only generically alive or returns unrelated JSON. That makes deployment health overstate service-specific OCR/vision readiness.
issues.notes: `probe_native_vision_runtime()` marks a runtime `available=True` as soon as `/vision/runtime` or `/healthz` returns any JSON object. It does not require a healthy status, a matching service entry, or any service-speci...

issues.title: Overall model readiness still hard-requires `llm_endpoint` even when the configured summary backend is local
issues.summary: `get_model_readiness_snapshot()` always includes `llm_endpoint` in the required readiness set. But `_probe_summary_readiness()` supports a local summary backend that does not depend on any remote LLM endpoint.
issues.impact: Ops/model readiness can still report the overall stack unready simply because `LLM_ENDPOINT` is absent, even when the active summary path is local and otherwise healthy. That keeps readiness tied to a remote subsystem that may not actually be part of the configured runtime.
issues.notes: `get_model_readiness_snapshot()` always includes `llm_endpoint` in the required readiness set. But `_probe_summary_readiness()` supports a local summary backend that does not depend on any remote LLM endpoint.

issues.title: Remote MT readiness still trusts any JSON response as a healthy translation runtime
issues.summary: `probe_remote_mt_runtime()` marks a remote MT endpoint `available=True` as soon as the base URL returns a JSON payload. It does not require a success status, a translation-specific capability marker, or any proof that the endpoint can actually serve MT requests.
issues.impact: NLLB remote readiness can still appear healthy when the endpoint is only generically alive or returns unrelated JSON. That makes translation-family health overstate actual remote MT availability.
issues.notes: `probe_remote_mt_runtime()` marks a remote MT endpoint `available=True` as soon as the base URL returns a JSON payload. It does not require a success status, a translation-specific capability marker, or any proof that...

issues.title: FTS invariant health checks are still schema-blind and can report false healthy
issues.summary: `get_fts_health_snapshot()` checks `pg_proc`, `pg_trigger`, and `pg_indexes` only by object name. It does not constrain the function, trigger, or index to the `chunks` table or the intended schema.
issues.impact: Startup and Ops can still report FTS healthy if same-named database objects exist elsewhere while the actual `chunks` search trigger/index/function are missing or detached. That is a false-healthy path for one of the repo’s core search invariants.
issues.notes: `get_fts_health_snapshot()` checks `pg_proc`, `pg_trigger`, and `pg_indexes` only by object name. It does not constrain the function, trigger, or index to the `chunks` table or the intended schema.

issues.title: Default model-status paths still report remote backends unavailable just because probing was skipped
issues.summary: The default `/ops/models` and `/health` paths call `get_model_readiness_snapshot(probe_load=False)`. In that mode, the remote/openai readiness helpers explicitly set `available=False` and only attach `note: "probe skipped"`.
issues.impact: The default observability surfaces can still show remote embedding, summary, and LLM subsystems as unavailable even when they are merely unprobed. That makes the standard non-probing health/model views pessimistic and misleading for remote-first deployments.
issues.notes: The default `/ops/models` and `/health` paths call `get_model_readiness_snapshot(probe_load=False)`. In that mode, the remote/openai readiness helpers explicitly set `available=False` and only attach `note: "probe ski...

issues.title: Recovered stale RUNNING jobs still burn an extra retry attempt before they are actually re-run
issues.summary: `recover_stale_jobs()` increments `attempt_count` when it resets a stale `RUNNING` job back to `QUEUED`, and `_atomic_claim_job()` increments `attempt_count` again when a worker later reclaims that same job.
issues.impact: Jobs recovered from stale `RUNNING` state lose two attempts for one actual retry cycle. That makes retry exhaustion and backoff harsher than intended and can push flaky jobs to FAILED/QUARANTINED earlier than the configured limits imply.
issues.notes: `recover_stale_jobs()` increments `attempt_count` when it resets a stale `RUNNING` job back to `QUEUED`, and `_atomic_claim_job()` increments `attempt_count` again when a worker later reclaims that same job.

issues.title: Dispatcher still drops the real correlation ID when building `JobContext`
issues.summary: `JobContext` has a `correlation_id` field, but `dispatch()` never passes one when it constructs the context. Every handler therefore sees the default `"worker"` value instead of the message/request correlation ID.
issues.impact: Handler-level tracing and any downstream logic that relies on `ctx.correlation_id` are still broken. The worker has the broker correlation ID in callback scope, but it never propagates that identifier into the execution context used by the job handlers.
issues.notes: `JobContext` has a `correlation_id` field, but `dispatch()` never passes one when it constructs the context. Every handler therefore sees the default `"worker"` value instead of the message/request correlation ID.

issues.title: MQ publishers still write correlation IDs only into headers, but DLQ preservation reads the AMQP property
issues.summary: `publish_job()` stores correlation IDs in `BasicProperties.headers["correlation_id"]` but does not populate `BasicProperties.correlation_id`. The DLQ/message-preservation helper clones only `properties.correlation_id`.
issues.impact: Broker messages can still lose their correlation ID when they are cloned/preserved outside the normal consumer path, because one side writes only the header while the other side reads only the AMQP property. That breaks trace continuity on ops/DLQ handling paths.
issues.notes: `publish_job()` stores correlation IDs in `BasicProperties.headers["correlation_id"]` but does not populate `BasicProperties.correlation_id`. The DLQ/message-preservation helper clones only `properties.correlation_id`.

issues.title: “Sync” runtime reconciliation is still not single-session or atomic
issues.summary: `reconcile_runtime_state_sync()` uses the caller’s DB session for stale-RUNNING recovery and outbox flush, but `recover_stale_queued_jobs()` always opens and commits its own `SessionLocal()` internally.
issues.impact: The ops “sync” maintenance path still presents itself as one coherent reconciliation pass, but one of its recovery steps commits in a separate session. That means the operation is not atomic, mixes transaction boundaries, and can leave partial maintenance effects behind if later steps fail.
issues.notes: `reconcile_runtime_state_sync()` uses the caller’s DB session for stale-RUNNING recovery and outbox flush, but `recover_stale_queued_jobs()` always opens and commits its own `SessionLocal()` internally.

issues.title: Direct-hit retrieval search still does per-result artifact and bucket lookups in Python
issues.summary: `run_retrieval_search()` handles direct artifact-ID and filename hits by fetching each candidate artifact through `get_visible_artifact_for_read()` and then separately checking `artifact_in_bucket()` for each one.
issues.impact: Exact ID/filename search still pays an avoidable N+1 cost on a hot retrieval path. Mounted explorer and search surfaces can become slower than necessary exactly when the query should be the cheapest path to resolve.
issues.notes: `run_retrieval_search()` handles direct artifact-ID and filename hits by fetching each candidate artifact through `get_visible_artifact_for_read()` and then separately checking `artifact_in_bucket()` for each one.

issues.title: Runtime-flag cache still uses wall-clock time instead of the file’s actual mtime
issues.summary: `_load_flags()` decides whether to reload by comparing `stat().st_mtime` against `_CACHE["mtime"]`, but `set_flags()` updates `_CACHE["mtime"]` with `time.time()` rather than the replaced file’s real mtime.
issues.impact: Same-process runtime flag reads can still miss near-simultaneous external updates when the filesystem mtime resolution lags behind `time.time()`. That leaves feature-flag state stale until a later write advances the on-disk mtime past the cached wall-clock value.
issues.notes: `_load_flags()` decides whether to reload by comparing `stat().st_mtime` against `_CACHE["mtime"]`, but `set_flags()` updates `_CACHE["mtime"]` with `time.time()` rather than the replaced file’s real mtime.

issues.title: Periodic stale-job recovery still runs unlocked from every worker process
issues.summary: `worker_loop()` runs periodic stale-job recovery in every worker process, but `recover_stale_jobs()` and `recover_stale_queued_jobs()` select stale jobs with plain `.all()` queries and no row locking.
issues.impact: Multiple worker processes can still recover the same stale RUNNING/QUEUED jobs concurrently, emitting duplicate requeue events and racing on outbox recreation. Recovery is therefore not coordinated cluster-wide even though it mutates shared job state.
issues.notes: `worker_loop()` runs periodic stale-job recovery in every worker process, but `recover_stale_jobs()` and `recover_stale_queued_jobs()` select stale jobs with plain `.all()` queries and no row locking.

issues.title: Reliability and startup-guard artifact counts still include soft-deleted artifacts
issues.summary: Both `get_db_reliability_snapshot()` and `enforce_startup_guard()` count all `Artifact` rows without filtering `is_deleted=False`, even though the model and visibility helpers treat soft-deleted artifacts as hidden.
issues.impact: Ops reliability and startup-guard checks can still overstate surviving corpus size and pass with a large number of soft-deleted artifacts. That weakens both health observability and restore-collapse detection.
issues.notes: Both `get_db_reliability_snapshot()` and `enforce_startup_guard()` count all `Artifact` rows without filtering `is_deleted=False`, even though the model and visibility helpers treat soft-deleted artifacts as hidden.

issues.title: Corrupted runtime-flags files still fail open to stale cached values after the first good read
issues.summary: `_load_flags()` raises on an invalid flags file only when there is no cached snapshot yet. Once `_CACHE["mtime"]` is nonzero, invalid JSON or non-dict content just logs an error and returns the previous cached data.
issues.impact: Runtime flag corruption can still be masked indefinitely inside a long-lived process. Operators see the old cached state rather than a hard failure, so bad flag files become easy to miss and harder to diagnose.
issues.notes: `_load_flags()` raises on an invalid flags file only when there is no cached snapshot yet. Once `_CACHE["mtime"]` is nonzero, invalid JSON or non-dict content just logs an error and returns the previous cached data.

issues.title: Direct filename-hit retrieval can underfill results and skip normal retrieval after Python-side scope filtering
issues.summary: `run_retrieval_search()` fetches a capped direct-filename candidate window, then applies visibility, scope, and bucket checks in Python. If any filtered filename hits remain, it returns them immediately instead of continuing into lexical/hybrid retrieval.
issues.impact: Queries with many out-of-scope or hidden filename matches can still return a thin, underfilled filename-only result set and never reach the normal lexical/hybrid search path. That makes exact-ish filename searches brittle and incomplete under scoped views.
issues.notes: `run_retrieval_search()` fetches a capped direct-filename candidate window, then applies visibility, scope, and bucket checks in Python. If any filtered filename hits remain, it returns them immediately instead of con...

issues.title: Report reads and false-alarm writes still widen to the whole case when both case and system scope are supplied
issues.summary: `_scoped_report_query()` returns immediately on `case_id` and ignores `system_id` whenever both are provided. The mounted report read/history/false-alarm routes all use that helper.
issues.impact: A caller that provides both `case_id` and `system_id` cannot actually constrain report reads or false-alarm actions to one system. If the mounted history path is corrected, the analyst report surface will still widen from the selected system to every report in the case.

issues.title: Manual report upload still accepts forged case/system ownership pairs
issues.summary: `/analysis/upload` writes whatever `case_id` and `system_id` the caller supplies directly into `Report` and never verifies that the system is linked to the case through `CaseSystem`.
issues.impact: A caller can create a `DONE` report attributed to an invalid case/system combination. That corrupts report ownership metadata, history, and any later scoped operations that trust the stored report scope.

issues.title: Advertised case-scoped reporting still collapses to a single-system contract
issues.summary: `AnalysisRequest` documents `scope` as `selection, case, system`, and the router only requires `case_id` for `scope="case"`, but `queue_report_generation()` still requires one resolved `system_id`, while the worker only knows how to load artifacts by explicit IDs or one `report.system_id`.
issues.impact: A true case-wide report is still impossible. Requests with only `case_id` fail, and requests with both `case_id` and `system_id` silently degrade into one-system report generation even though systems are explicitly shareable across cases.
issues.notes: `AnalysisRequest` documents `scope` as `selection, case, system`, and the router only requires `case_id` for `scope="case"`, but `queue_report_generation()` still requires one resolved `system_id`, while the worker on...

issues.title: Settings KB term create/toggle flows still omit the required KB scope entirely
issues.summary: The mounted KB term admin UI still posts create/update payloads without `case_id` or `bucket_id`, but both backend routes call `require_kb_scope()` before mutating terms.
issues.impact: The visible “Save Term” and enabled-toggle actions in Settings are still dead-end controls. Even after fixing the existing list-shape issues, analysts cannot create or update KB terms from the mounted UI because every mutation request fails validation before it reaches term logic.

issues.title: This ledger’s doc-drift section still contains stale evidence paths under `Roadmap/Docs/`
issues.summary: Many doc-drift findings in this ledger still cite `Roadmap/Docs/...` files that do not exist in the current repo layout, while the live documentation tree is rooted under `docs/`.
issues.impact: A substantial slice of the ledger is harder to re-verify because the evidence blocks point at moved or absent files. Some of those findings may still be substantively correct, but the current citations are no longer trustworthy enough for a clean source-backed audit pass.
issues.notes: Many doc-drift findings in this ledger still cite `Roadmap/Docs/...` files that do not exist in the current repo layout, while the live documentation tree is rooted under `docs/`.

issues.title: Thread merge is still an unscoped raw-UUID mutation route
issues.summary: `POST /threads/merge` accepts bundle UUIDs directly, loads target and source bundles by raw ID, and never requires or validates caller `case_id` or `system_id`. The route only checks whether the bundles match each other, not whether they match any caller-authorized scope.
issues.impact: Anyone who can obtain thread bundle IDs can still merge thread bundles across case or system boundaries they should not control, as long as the bundles are internally scope-consistent. This is a live cross-scope mutation path, not just another legacy read leak.

issues.title: Thread list and timeline still expose bundle metadata after every member artifact becomes invisible
issues.summary: Both thread read routes load `AnalysisBundle` rows before applying artifact visibility, then only filter member items when counting or materializing the timeline. Bundles with zero visible artifacts are still returned with IDs, names, scope metadata, and timestamps.
issues.impact: Deleted, quarantined, or otherwise hidden thread members can collapse to `item_count: 0` or an empty timeline while the enclosing bundle remains discoverable. That leaks stale investigation structure and leaves hidden bundles targetable by the still-unscoped merge route.
issues.notes: Both thread read routes load `AnalysisBundle` rows before applying artifact visibility, then only filter member items when counting or materializing the timeline. Bundles with zero visible artifacts are still returned...

issues.title: Settings KB scope selector is still fake for the mounted Terms/Canonical/OSINT tabs
issues.summary: `SettingsPage` persists `kbCaseId` in component state and URL state, but only the candidate-review fetches use it. The mounted Terms tab still calls `/kb/terms` without `case_id` or `bucket_id` even though the backend requires KB scope, while the Canonical and OSINT tabs also ignore the visible case selector and read their global endpoints unscoped.
issues.impact: The visible KB case selector only affects the candidate-review tabs. The Terms tab still fails required-scope validation, and the Canonical/OSINT tabs still widen to global data even when the user believes they are reviewing a scoped case KB surface.

issues.title: Process-thread list and detail routes still disagree on case-scope enforcement
issues.summary: `GET /processes/` ignores `ENFORCE_CASE_AUTH` and silently falls back to `ProcessThread.case_id IS NULL` when no `case_id` is supplied, while `GET /processes/{thread_id}/kanban` calls `_get_thread_or_404()` and requires explicit `case_id` whenever case auth is enabled. The remaining UI helper for process threads also never sends `case_id`.
issues.impact: Under `ENFORCE_CASE_AUTH`, clients still get a list response with only global threads while case-scoped process threads remain undiscoverable unless callers already know a thread UUID and pass the right `case_id`. That leaves the API internally inconsistent and can still leak any global process-thread inventory.
issues.notes: `GET /processes/` ignores `ENFORCE_CASE_AUTH` and silently falls back to `ProcessThread.case_id IS NULL` when no `case_id` is supplied, while `GET /processes/{thread_id}/kanban` calls `_get_thread_or_404()` and requir...

issues.title: Canonical creation still ignores the selected KB case and would default to global entities
issues.summary: `SettingsPage` exposes a KB case selector, but the canonical-create form state has no `case_id`, and `handleCanonicalCreate()` posts `newCanonical` unchanged. The backend canonical-create route does support optional `case_id` and persists it when present.
issues.impact: Even if the existing canonical-form shape bug is fixed, the mounted Settings flow would still create global canonical entities instead of case-local ones. That makes the visible KB case selector misleading and risks polluting global canonical state with scoped analyst intent.
issues.notes: `SettingsPage` exposes a KB case selector, but the canonical-create form state has no `case_id`, and `handleCanonicalCreate()` posts `newCanonical` unchanged. The backend canonical-create route does support optional `...

issues.title: Triage analyze/get routes still bypass case-scope enforcement
issues.summary: Both `/api/triage/analyze/{artifact_id}` and `/api/triage/{artifact_id}` still resolve artifacts through `get_visible_artifact_for_read()`, which only checks deleted/security visibility and never checks `Batch.case_id` or any caller scope.
issues.impact: Under `ENFORCE_CASE_AUTH`, any caller who knows an artifact UUID can still read or overwrite triage data for another case through this mounted router. That is a live cross-case read/write surface, not just missing UI polish.

issues.title: Manual triage still overwrites pipeline-owned triage metadata
issues.summary: `analyze_artifact()` replaces `artifact.triage_result` with the narrow `run_triage_analysis()` payload instead of merging it with the existing JSON. That payload only contains PE/Office/YARA-style fields, so any prior pipeline-owned keys are discarded on manual re-analysis.
issues.impact: Re-running manual triage on an already processed artifact can silently drop persisted fields such as `doc_classification` and `content_simhash`. That breaks downstream classification and similarity behavior on the same artifact even though the analyst only intended to refresh security triage.

issues.title: Keylog summary still drops the case filter whenever a system is supplied
issues.summary: The mounted Keylogs panel sends both `case_id` and `system_id`, but `/keylogs/summary` uses an `elif` chain, so providing a system automatically discards the case filter instead of applying both constraints together.
issues.impact: Because systems are explicitly shareable across cases, a case-scoped keylog review can still aggregate artifacts from other cases on the same system. That violates the workspace-boundary discipline the roadmap docs describe for closed evidence surfaces such as keylogs.

issues.title: False-alarm flag appends can still report success without persisting
issues.summary: `POST /analysis/false_alarm` mutates the existing `false_alarm_flags` list in place and then assigns the same object back to a plain `JSON` column. SQLAlchemy does not reliably mark that as changed once the list already exists.
issues.impact: The first false-alarm flag may persist, but later additions are at risk of being dropped while the route still returns `{"status": "flagged"}`. That leaves analyst feedback looking accepted even when it was not durably saved.
issues.notes: `POST /analysis/false_alarm` mutates the existing `false_alarm_flags` list in place and then assigns the same object back to a plain `JSON` column. SQLAlchemy does not reliably mark that as changed once the list alrea...

issues.title: `/api/triage` still strips `docx_info` from Office triage responses
issues.summary: `run_triage_analysis()` returns `docx_info`, and the roadmap output docs describe it as part of `triage_result`, but the mounted triage router uses a `TriageReport` response model that omits `docx_info`. FastAPI therefore filters the Office integrity payload out of both analyze and get responses.
issues.impact: The live triage API hides Bangla-font and OOXML integrity signals that the worker and docs treat as first-class triage output. Office analysts can persist those signals in DB and see them elsewhere, but cannot retrieve them through the dedicated triage surface.
issues.notes: `run_triage_analysis()` returns `docx_info`, and the roadmap output docs describe it as part of `triage_result`, but the mounted triage router uses a `TriageReport` response model that omits `docx_info`. FastAPI there...

issues.title: Translation can leave derived outputs behind after the job is rolled back
issues.summary: `persist_translation_result()` writes translated files, layout JSON, table JSON, variant outputs, chunks, and triage metadata before it queues follow-on embedding/NER/classification work. If any later step raises, the dispatcher rolls back the DB transaction but cannot roll back the derived files already written to disk.
issues.impact: A failed translation job can still leave fresh translated artifacts on disk with no committed chunk/triage/job lineage. That breaks provenance and makes the derived store disagree with the database about whether translation actually succeeded.
issues.notes: `persist_translation_result()` writes translated files, layout JSON, table JSON, variant outputs, chunks, and triage metadata before it queues follow-on embedding/NER/classification work. If any later step raises, the...

issues.title: Security-state compatibility timestamps can fail to persist on `triage_result`
issues.summary: `apply_security_state()` mutates the existing `triage_result` dict in place and assigns the same object back to a plain `JSONB` column. SQLAlchemy does not reliably mark that JSON as dirty, so `nat_checked_at` and `nat_state_updated_at` can be lost even when the helper returns success.
issues.impact: Security status reads can return stale `updated_at` values, and legacy NAT compatibility timestamps can silently fail to advance after triage or override actions. That makes the security timeline less trustworthy even when the primary `security_state` changed correctly.
issues.notes: `apply_security_state()` mutates the existing `triage_result` dict in place and assigns the same object back to a plain `JSONB` column. SQLAlchemy does not reliably mark that JSON as dirty, so `nat_checked_at` and `na...

issues.title: Gap-analysis doc still points the vault contract at a removed legacy path
issues.summary: The gap-analysis roadmap still lists the content-addressed vault implementation at `common/vault.py`, while the live runtime helper is `common/storage/vault.py` and the storage-boundary doc already treats vault as a current shared storage helper surface.
issues.impact: The roadmap still sends reviewers to a stale path for one of the repo’s core storage contracts. That makes storage/runtime verification noisier and weakens the docs’ role as a reliable architecture map during audits.
issues.notes: The gap-analysis roadmap still lists the content-addressed vault implementation at `common/vault.py`, while the live runtime helper is `common/storage/vault.py` and the storage-boundary doc already treats vault as a c...

issues.title: API keys are still accepted and emitted through URL query parameters
issues.summary: The shared auth guard accepts `api_key` from the query string, and the UI helper `withApiKey()` appends the active API key directly into artifact/preview/raw URLs. The repo guidance, however, documents `X-API-Key` as the required contract for `/api/*`.
issues.impact: Long-lived API keys can leak into browser history, copied URLs, access logs, and intermediary tooling via the request URL instead of staying in headers. This weakens the auth boundary on every mounted preview/raw surface that relies on `withApiKey()`.

issues.title: Repo still carries a second non-canonical vault implementation under `api/src/services`
issues.summary: The documented storage contract says shared vault helpers live under `common/storage/`, and all live imports use that canonical helper, but the repo still carries a separate `api/src/services/vault.py` implementation with a weaker feature set.
issues.impact: This is dead duplicate logic around a core storage boundary. If it gets reused accidentally, it bypasses the canonical vault helper’s stricter validation and atomic-write behavior, and it keeps the repo’s storage ownership story noisier than the docs claim.
issues.notes: The documented storage contract says shared vault helpers live under `common/storage/`, and all live imports use that canonical helper, but the repo still carries a separate `api/src/services/vault.py` implementation ...

issues.title: Outbox flush still ignores the persisted correlation ID when it republishes jobs
issues.summary: `JobPublisher` persists `correlation_id` inside `JobOutbox.payload`, but `flush_outbox()` republishes using only `job_id`, `queue_name`, and `priority`. `publish_job()` then falls back to the flusher thread’s ambient correlation context instead of the original request/job correlation ID.
issues.impact: Jobs published later by the outbox flusher or recovery flow lose their original correlation lineage and get republished under a new/flusher-local correlation ID. That weakens end-to-end tracing exactly on the degraded-delivery paths where correlation is most valuable.
issues.notes: `JobPublisher` persists `correlation_id` inside `JobOutbox.payload`, but `flush_outbox()` republishes using only `job_id`, `queue_name`, and `priority`. `publish_job()` then falls back to the flusher thread’s ambient ...

issues.title: Bucket-scoped artifact actions still drop the bucket boundary before queued deep analysis runs
issues.summary: The mounted artifact and orchestrator action routes validate `bucket_id` against the target artifact, but they build `ActionOptions` without any bucket payload. The orchestrator service only forwards `options.payload`, and deep analysis derives its grounding bucket exclusively from `job.payload`.
issues.impact: A caller can launch a bucket-scoped action from the mounted surfaces and still get deep-analysis grounding from artifacts outside the selected bucket, because the queued job runs with case/system scope only. That widens evidence scope across the exact closed-review boundary the bucket selector is supposed to enforce.

issues.title: Re-running deep analysis can still report success without persisting refreshed `analysis_result`
issues.summary: `process_deep_analysis_task()` starts from `artifact.analysis_result or {}`, mutates that dict in place, and assigns the same object back to `Artifact.analysis_result`, which is a plain `JSON` column.
issues.impact: The first deep-analysis write usually persists, but later reruns on an artifact that already has `analysis_result` can silently drop refreshed summary, model, provenance, or grounding data while the job still finishes `DONE`. That leaves analyst-visible deep-analysis output stale even after a successful rerun.
issues.notes: `process_deep_analysis_task()` starts from `artifact.analysis_result or {}`, mutates that dict in place, and assigns the same object back to `Artifact.analysis_result`, which is a plain `JSON` column.

issues.title: Bucket CRUD and membership routes are still deployment-global and bypass case enforcement
issues.summary: The live `/buckets` router takes no `case_id` on list/create/update/delete/membership routes, `Bucket` itself has no case ownership column, and artifact membership resolution still goes through `get_visible_artifact_for_read()`, which only checks deleted/security visibility.
issues.impact: Any caller with API access can still enumerate, rename, delete, and repopulate buckets across case boundaries, and can attach or remove artifacts from other cases if they know the IDs. That turns a supposed focused-review scope mechanism into a global cross-case mutation surface.

issues.title: CIM identity-resolution logs still default to global cross-case reads
issues.summary: `IdentityResolutionLog` is a case-owned model, and neighboring CIM list routes require `case_id`, but `/cim/identity-resolution/logs` keeps `case_id` optional and starts from an unscoped table query.
issues.impact: A caller can still pull merge/split audit history across every case by omitting `case_id`, even though the rest of the CIM list surface is case-scoped. That leaks cross-case analyst reasoning and weakens the case-boundary contract on a live audit trail endpoint.
issues.notes: `IdentityResolutionLog` is a case-owned model, and neighboring CIM list routes require `case_id`, but `/cim/identity-resolution/logs` keeps `case_id` optional and starts from an unscoped table query.

issues.title: CIM actor merge/split routes still mutate by raw actor UUID without caller case scope
issues.summary: The actor detail route already uses `_require_case_scoped_detail()`, but the live merge and split routes still load actors by raw UUID only and then infer the case from those rows. They never require the caller to prove access to that case.
issues.impact: Anyone who can discover actor UUIDs can still merge or split identity records inside another case. That is a live cross-case mutation surface on CIM entities, not just another detail-read inconsistency.

issues.title: Artifact-registration metadata updates can still report success without persisting
issues.summary: `create_or_update_artifact_registration()` merges new metadata by mutating the existing `registration.metadata_` dict in place and then assigning the same object back to a plain `JSONB` column.
issues.impact: When an existing registration row is updated, refreshed provenance such as duplicate-hash context can be dropped silently even though the intake path returns success. That leaves the durable registration ledger stale exactly where analysts expect path-level appearance history to stay accurate.
issues.notes: `create_or_update_artifact_registration()` merges new metadata by mutating the existing `registration.metadata_` dict in place and then assigning the same object back to a plain `JSONB` column.

issues.title: Snapshot restore still leaves hypotheses from the newer case state behind
issues.summary: `SnapshotEngine` captures hypotheses into `snapshot_data`, but the live restore path only deletes and rebuilds actors, assets, events, and claims. It never deletes or recreates `Hypothesis` rows, yet the API still reports the case as successfully restored.
issues.impact: Restoring a case snapshot produces a mixed state: actors/assets/events/claims roll back, but later hypotheses remain in place with stale evidence pointers and confidence context from the post-snapshot world. The route returns `"status": "restored"` even though the semantic case state was not actually restored.

issues.title: Thread clustering still hard-fails when the local embedding model is unavailable
issues.summary: `cluster_threads()` only clusters already-persisted `Artifact.embedding` vectors with DBSCAN, but it still calls `get_model()` first and marks the job `FAILED` when no local SentenceTransformer model is loaded.
issues.impact: Deployments that generate embeddings remotely, or simply do not ship the local sentence-transformer runtime on the clustering worker, can still build vectors successfully but then never form threads. Instead of a clean skip or clustering from stored vectors, the job is recorded as a failure for a dependency it does not actually need at runtime.
issues.notes: Thread clustering still hard-fails when the local embedding model is unavailable

issues.title: Meta-synthesis reports still leak cross-case history on shared systems
issues.summary: The worker correctly narrows the artifact set to `report.case_id` when a report is case-scoped, but the `META_SYNTHESIZE` history lookup later drops that case filter and checks only `system_id + sha256`. Because systems are explicitly shareable across cases, the generated “history” section can pull sightings from other cases on the same system.
issues.impact: An analyst can run a case-scoped Meta Synthesis report and still receive cross-case historical sightings in the generated narrative. That is a live evidence-boundary leak inside report content, not just a query-listing bug.

issues.title: Meta-synthesis still labels same-batch duplicates as prior historical appearances
issues.summary: `_generate_meta_synthesis()` says it is finding matches from the same system but a different batch, yet the query only excludes the current artifact ID. Other artifacts from the current ingest batch with the same SHA256 are therefore counted as “appeared previously”.
issues.impact: The report can overstate file history by treating contemporaneous duplicates in the same batch as older sightings. That makes the “historical matches detected” section misleading even when scope is otherwise correct.
issues.notes: Meta-synthesis still labels same-batch duplicates as prior historical appearances

issues.title: OCR quick action still drops every engine-preference hint
issues.summary: Both live action request models expose `prefer_surya`, `prefer_tesseract`, and `prefer_qwen_vl`, and both routers pass them into `ActionOptions`, but the `ocr_quick` branch only forwards `force_ocr` and `suppress_structured_escalation`. On the worker side, `build_router_request()` also hard-wires `prefer_tesseract=True` for every `OCR_QUICK` job.
issues.impact: A caller can request OCR quick with Surya, Tesseract, or Qwen-VL preferences and still get the default quick OCR lane instead. The API contract advertises engine steering that the runtime does not honor, which makes OCR troubleshooting and reproducible reruns harder.
issues.notes: OCR quick action still drops every engine-preference hint

issues.title: Temporal anomaly detection still drops zero-confidence events
issues.summary: `detect_anomalies()` builds its low-confidence list with `if e.confidence and e.confidence < 0.5`, so events with confidence exactly `0.0` are treated as falsy and never reported as `LOW_CONFIDENCE`.
issues.impact: The mounted temporal anomalies endpoint can suppress the worst-confidence extracted events from analyst review. A case can therefore look cleaner than it is precisely when confidence collapsed to zero.
issues.notes: Temporal anomaly detection still drops zero-confidence events

issues.title: Temporal spike detection still ignores quiet days in its baseline
issues.summary: `detect_event_spikes()` only counts dates that already have at least one event and then computes mean and standard deviation from that reduced set. It never inserts zero-count days across the lookback window before scoring spikes.
issues.impact: Sparse cases with one short burst after many quiet days can fail to register as spikes because the baseline is computed only across already-active days. That makes the live spike endpoint systematically under-detect intermittent surges.
issues.notes: Temporal spike detection still ignores quiet days in its baseline

issues.title: Re-running `CONTENT_CLASSIFY` still overwrites analyst classification overrides
issues.summary: The override route stores the analyst’s chosen final classification in the legacy `classification_*` fields and marks the override with `content_classification_override_*`. Later `CONTENT_CLASSIFY` runs then unconditionally overwrite those same `classification_*` fields with detected output, even though the classification service treats those fields as the final override source whenever an override exists.
issues.impact: An analyst can apply a manual classification override and later lose it silently when ingestion, OCR, or translation queues another `CONTENT_CLASSIFY` job. The artifact will still look like it has an override, but the stored final level/label will have been replaced with machine output.

issues.title: `TriagePanel` is still an orphaned UI surface wired to the legacy triage route
issues.summary: `TriagePanel` is no longer imported anywhere in the live UI, but it still carries a standalone triage workflow that posts directly to `/triage/analyze/{artifact_id}` and renders the older entropy/YARA report shape.
issues.impact: This file is still authored-file and maintenance cost without any runtime capability. It also preserves an older analyst workflow contract that now drifts separately from the mounted preview/orchestrator surfaces.
issues.notes: `TriagePanel` is no longer imported anywhere in the live UI, but it still carries a standalone triage workflow that posts directly to `/triage/analyze/{artifact_id}` and renders the older entropy/YARA report shape.

issues.title: `BucketPanel` is still an orphaned mock-only bucket surface
issues.summary: `BucketPanel` is no longer imported anywhere in the live UI, and the component itself never talks to the backend. It only mutates local React state and even labels its interaction as a simulated MVP flow.
issues.impact: The repo is still paying for a dead bucket UI that cannot represent real case/bucket state. It is a straightforward deletion/cleanup candidate in the authored-file-count reduction pass.
issues.notes: `BucketPanel` is no longer imported anywhere in the live UI, and the component itself never talks to the backend. It only mutates local React state and even labels its interaction as a simulated MVP flow.

issues.title: `AnalysisQueue` is still an orphaned selection buffer mislabeled as a real queue
issues.summary: `AnalysisQueue` is no longer imported anywhere in the live UI, and the component never reads backend queue state. It only renders the caller’s local `selectedIds` array and labels that transient selection buffer as “Analysis Queue”.
issues.impact: This file is dead UI surface plus stale terminology. Keeping it around makes the codebase look like it has a mounted queue panel when it does not, and it should be part of the deletion set for the under-200-file reduction plan.
issues.notes: `AnalysisQueue` is no longer imported anywhere in the live UI, and the component never reads backend queue state. It only renders the caller’s local `selectedIds` array and labels that transient selection buffer as “A...

issues.title: Analyst-safe artifact telemetry still exposes browser request metadata
issues.summary: Manual artifact events attach `origin`, `referer`, and full `user_agent` from the incoming HTTP request. The mounted artifact events and telemetry routes then return those event payloads after only redacting `client_host`, `vault_path`, and `*_path` keys, so the browser metadata remains visible to analysts.
issues.impact: The mounted artifact telemetry/event surfaces can leak analyst workstation/browser context such as internal referer URLs and user-agent strings while still presenting themselves as “analyst-safe.” That is a live metadata exposure on normal artifact review routes.
issues.notes: Analyst-safe artifact telemetry still exposes browser request metadata

issues.title: `api/src` still contains side-effectful one-off debug scripts inside the package tree
issues.summary: Several standalone troubleshooting scripts still live under `api/src`, and some of them execute database queries immediately at import time instead of behind a `__main__` guard. None are referenced anywhere else in the repo.
issues.impact: This cluster is dead authored-file cost and an avoidable package-tree hazard. Accidental imports can still open DB sessions and run raw queries at import time, and the files should be moved out of `api/src` or deleted as part of the reduction pass.
issues.notes: Several standalone troubleshooting scripts still live under `api/src`, and some of them execute database queries immediately at import time instead of behind a `__main__` guard. None are referenced anywhere else in th...

issues.title: Repo still carries an unused top-level re-export shim layer for moved common/runtime modules
issues.summary: The codebase still includes multiple one-line `import *` wrapper files that only re-export newer canonical modules from `common.runtime`, `common.storage`, or `common.translation`. Repo-wide search shows the product code no longer imports these shims.
issues.impact: This layer keeps parallel ownership paths alive even after the repo moved to canonical `common.runtime/*`, `common.storage/*`, and `common.translation/*` modules. It adds authored-file count and keeps consolidation harder because readers still see multiple apparent import homes for the same code.
issues.notes: The codebase still includes multiple one-line `import *` wrapper files that only re-export newer canonical modules from `common.runtime`, `common.storage`, or `common.translation`. Repo-wide search shows the product c...
